programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team flies to Israel, but receives a hostile reception from Ziva's father, who is the head of intelligence agency Mossad. As events unfold, Gibbs learns that Ziva was romantically involved with Rivkin and finds himself forced to make a tough decision. Guest starring Michael Nouri, alongside Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A Navy officer dies following a confrontation involving a dancer and a Sudanese emigre at a strip club. However, when investigations reveal he was a code-breaker involved in naval intelligence, Callen and Sam dig deeper and gradually unearth links to Safar, a Libyan-born terrorist known to recruit on behalf of al-Qaeda. Henri Lubatti guest stars

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called to the scene of a fatal car crash involving marine lance corporal Chad Dunn and student Lyla Cutwright. Duckie notices that the woman's injuries do not match the specifics of the accident and there are no skidmarks found at the scene, leading Gibbs to declare they possibly have a murder and a suicide on their hands. Abby calls in sick for the first time after the case stirs up a traumatic incident from her childhood. Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team capture computer hacker Ajay Khan, who was recently responsible for hacking into NCIS's supposedly ultra-secure multiple-threat alert centre. They believe he could hold the key to tracking down the most-wanted cyber terrorist in the world, who may be plotting an attack using a bomb containing a strain of the Ebola virus. When Khan refuses to co-operate, Ziva and DiNozzo take him to Guantanamo Bay to be placed with people he had previously betrayed. Guest starring Vik Sahay (Chuck)

Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: After a mid-series break, the drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects returns. Sam and Callen investigate when five men wearing US Marine uniforms are found dead in a warehouse and discover they were killed by a lethal toxin that could be used as a bioweapon. Initial inquiries lead them to a former military chemist who now has Alzheimer's, while Callen recognises one of the victims as someone who used to work as hired muscle for the well-connected owner of a strip joint. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.desc: The promise of romance is put on hold when news of another murder interrupts Luther's date with Mary. Determined to predict the copycat fetishist's next move, he throws himself into a pile of cold case files and after his public disagreement with Ripley, he orders the sergeant to investigate the killing of Jared Cass on his own. Meanwhile, a care-home cancer patient receives a chilling gift from a visitor, and three girls look forward to a night in - unaware of the danger lurking in their home. Crime drama, starring Idris Elba, Warren Brown and Ned Dennehy

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reopens the case of criminal Mark Johnson, who was killed in a fire at London's Union club in 1996. The original investigation was hampered by silence among witnesses and associates, but with new information suggesting the blaze was a targeted arson attack, the detectives hope they will have more success. Drama, guest starring Roy Marsden, Martin Marquez and Tom Watt

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: In a grim turn of events, the tooth of the abducted woman is found in the debris from a wood chipper, while Walker's ex-wife's boyfriend turns psychotic

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: A man admitted to hospital after a traffic accident turns out to be one of two children who disappeared without trace 15 years previously. At the time, a nationwide manhunt failed to produce any answers, and now the cold case squad investigates where the missing boy has been all this time and whether his twin sister is still alive. Detective drama, starring Trevor Eve

programme.title: The 39 Steps
programme.desc: Rupert Penry-Jones stars in this adaptation of John Buchan's classic novel, set in 1914. Engineer Richard Hannay is bored and restless in London - until he meets Scudder, who claims to be a British spy. When the agent is killed in his flat, Hannay is thrown headlong into adventure, forced to go on the run under suspicion of murder. Fleeing to Scotland, with police and spies on his tail, he teams up with feisty suffragette Victoria Sinclair, and although they fail to hit it off, the couple become increasingly reliant on each other as they race to unmask a traitor and save Great Britain from German invasion. Action thriller, co-starring Eddie Marsan and Lydia Leonard

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The former leader of an Irish Republican group comes forward with information regarding the abduction and subsequent disappearance of an 18-year-old woman in 1983, prompting the team to reopen the case. The reformed dissident claims the rebels used the kidnapping to raise their profile, but his subsequent reinvention as a politician and man of peace leaves Sandra and her colleagues suspicious of his motives. They also investigate whether the arms-manufacturing activities of the victim's father could have made her a target. Crime drama, guest starring Sian Phillips and Annette Crosbie, with Amanda Redman and James Bolam

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: First of two-part crime drama from the prolific pen of Lynda La Plante. DCI Roisin Connor is called in to investigate the gruesome discovery of a woman's severed hand in the River Thames, and soon finds herself pitted against rival officer Chief Superintendent Mike Walker as they are drawn into a deadly game with a psychotic killer. Victoria Smurfit and David Hayman star

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A recently released convict comes under suspicion when a warden at his parole hostel is murdered, but when the cold case squad becomes involved, it transpires the former prisoner may have been the intended target rather than the perpetrator, and that this killing may be connected to previous, unsolved crimes. Nigel Terry and Kerry Fox guest star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: A hang-gliding enthusiast crashes down in the village, mistakenly believing that he's landed in France, thus breaking the long-distance record. The gang tries to compensate for his disappointment by making him the best at something else - identifying a strange cat creature lurking in the woods. Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Brian Murphy star

programme.title: Inspector Alleyn Mysteries
programme.desc: An investigation gets under way when barrister Luke Watchman dies of cyanide poisoning during his annual holiday at an inn. Crime drama, starring Patrick Malahide, Kevin McNally and William Simons

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The gun used to kill a boxer is discovered at the scene of an armed robbery 15 years later, prompting the team to reinvestigate the case. Twenty-one-year-old Eddie Mayfair was shot dead a week after winning an important bout, which left his opponent seriously injured and unable to carry on his career. The detectives initially suspect revenge as a motive, but the case is complicated when they uncover the rivalries and money-making aspects of the sport. Guest starring Maggie O'Neill (Shameless) and Chloe Howman (Casualty)

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Conclusion of the two-part crime drama. Connor and Walker fear for the children's safety as their suspicions about Harwood are confirmed, and when they finally catch up with him it seems he has lost his mind. David Hayman, Victoria Smurfit and Charles Dance star

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The cold case squad investigates the grisly unsolved murder of a headmaster, when another man is found killed in very similar circumstances, and they begin to suspect the victims may have been targeted because of their sexuality. However, their efforts are complicated by the Organised Crime Unit, who are also assigned to the case and have very different ideas about why these men were killed. Phil Daniels guest stars

programme.desc: Frankie is feeling downbeat so Zack offers to take her on a driving lesson - but his good deed has terrible consequences. Kat asks Denise to reconsider letting Phil into Raymond's life, Patrick returns the things that Isaac stole to a shocked Ruby and Martin, and Peter resolves to help a heartbroken Bobby move on with his life

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and David and Harry Rich come up with competing designs for a Bristol couple on a budget of £6,500, The couple want to transform their large, disordered and overgrown garden into an urban suntrap reminiscent of their holidays in the Mediterranean

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how Britain turned its sporting reputation around over 16 years to triumph at the 2012 Olympics. The first episode begins with the UK's poor performance in the 1996 games at Atlanta, and how the Government diverted National Lottery funding to provide the best possible training for the nation's athletes. Featuring contributions from Linford Christie and Kelly Holmes

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: The case of Michaella McCollum, a teenager from Northern Ireland who formed a relationship with a drug trafficker during her first trip abroad in Ibiza. He persuaded her to assist him in smuggling drugs for his cartel contacts, and just a few weeks later, she was arrested at Lima airport in Peru with more than a million pounds worth of cocaine in her suitcase

programme.title: Cristiano Ronaldo: Impossible to Ignore
programme.desc: The story of Portuguese football star Cristiano Ronaldo's life and career, revealing how he became the world's biggest sporting brand

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor Stephen Mangan hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Janey Godley and journalist Helen Lewis joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a Victorian semi in London's Forest Hill, a terraced house in Stoke-on-Trent, and a piece of land in Staffordshire

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate an infamous Scottish murder case from 1862 where a compelling alternative suspect could have been the true killer

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, following the work of those providing emergency and medical care to the Shetland Islands' 23,000 residents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and John Cameron help the red and blue teams search for valuable items in Leominster, Herefordshire, in the hope of making a profit at auction. Plus, host Anita Manning finds out more about Worcester's saucy secret

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day eight of the tournament at the All England Club, where the women's singles quarter-finals are scheduled to be played. British number one Johanna Konta missed out on a second semi-final in three years at this stage in 2019 when she was beaten 7-6, 6-1 by the Czech Republic's Barbora Strycova, while eventual champion Simona Halep recorded a victory by the exact same scoreline against Zhang Shuai

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Italy v Spain (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the opening semi-final, as the winners of the second and first quarter-finals meet at Wembley Stadium in a repeat of the 2012 final. The Azzurri, led by former Manchester City boss Roberto Mancini, have looked strong throughout the tournament. They breezed through the group stage with maximum points and no goals conceded, and have overcome Austria and Belgium by consecutive 2-1 scorelines to reach the last four. Spain's route to the semis has been a rockier one, with Luis Enrique's side finishing second in Group E, and winning a remarkable last-16 game with Croatia by a 5-3 scoreline. They then needed a penalty shootout to overcome Switzerland in the quarter-finals, but all of that will be forgotten ahead of this clash with another of Europe's most successful nations

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Peter Crouch, Maya Jama and Alex Horne are back for more live Euros fun and the stakes are high as the first of the semi-final gets under way. Tonight they'll be joined by stars of People Just Do Nothing, Kurupt FM, for the usual mix of chat and games. Plus, there will be live music by the Horne Section

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Italy v Spain. Action from the opening semi-final, as the winners of the second and first quarter-finals met at Wembley Stadium

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma and Norman attempt to reconnect as they plan for the future, while Dylan and Romero decide to take action for Norman's own good

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman tries to put together a special event for Norma, and Romero is confronted with the consequences of his actions

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Italy v Spain. A chance to see the opening semi-final at Wembley Stadium, as the sides contested a place in the showpiece match

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of a former commercial unit in Madeley in Staffordshire, a fire-damaged house in Wolverhampton and a former care home in Ilkeston in Derbyshire

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein explore the controversial killing of a police officer which sparked the round-up of dozens of suspects and ended with three men going to the gallows

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Charles Hanson help two teams look for items at Oswestry Showground in Shropshire, with presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp takes a trip along Pontcysyllte aqueduct

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day nine of the tournament at the All England Club, featuring action from the outside courts

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day nine of the tournament at the All England Club, where the men's singles quarter-finals are scheduled to be played. Novak Djokovic and Roger Federer demonstrated why they were still the top two seeds after many years at the pinnacle of the game when they recorded victories over David Goffin and Kei Nishikori respectively at this stage in 2019, and the two eventually met in the final, with Djokovic prevailing

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Coverage of the second semi-final (Kick-off 8.00pm), as the winners of the fourth and third quarter-finals meet at Wembley Stadium. A brace by Antoine Griezmann put hosts France in the final at the expense of Germany at this stage of the 2016 tournament, with Didier Deschamps' men proving to be the more clinical and claim a fairly comfortable victory despite the Germans having 65% of the possession. The sides also met in the group stage of this tournament, with the French again prevailing, this time 1-0 courtesy of Mats Hummels' own goal

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Peter Crouch, Maya Jama and Alex Horne are joined by former England star Alex Scott and comedian Jack Whitehall for the last of their live shows, as they react to the concluding semi-final and look forward to the final

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: England v Denmark. Action from the second semi-final, as the winners of the fourth and third quarter-finals met at Wembley Stadium

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how Britain turned its sporting reputation around over 16 years to triumph at the 2012 Olympics. The first episode begins with the UK's poor performance in the 1996 games at Atlanta, and how the Government diverted National Lottery funding to provide the best possible training for the nation's athletes. Featuring contributions from Linford Christie and Kelly Holmes

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: A chance to see the second semi-final at Wembley Stadium, as the line-up for Sunday's showpiece match was confirmed

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reexamine a complex case from Victorian Britain that featured four prime suspects, two trials, and ultimately just one man hanged for the murder of his neighbour. In 1882, 43-year old shop-keeper Harriet Candler was killed in a brutal attack, with her property ransacked

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Vintage musical instrument expert Pete Woods takes on a tarnished, dented saxophone that once played to lift people's spirits during the Blitz, but has not made a sound in more than 40 years. Silversmith Brenton West's skills are tested when he is handed an 80-year-old brass kettle in desperate need of professional attention. Saddler Suzie Fletcher restores a leather shaving mirror used by troops in the trenches of the First World War, and metal-worker Dominic Chinea sharpens up his act with a vintage carpenter's fret saw that has seen better days

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including Hull Kingston Rovers v Hull FC and Castleford Tigers v St Helens

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of last week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day seven of the tournament at the All England Club, where fourth-round matches in the men's and women's singles are scheduled to be played, with places in the quarter-finals up for grabs. Rafael Nadal was among the victors at this stage in 2019, impressing on Centre Court with a 6-2, 6-2, 6-2 victory over Joao Sousa, but the two-time champion was ruled out of this year's tournament. Over on the No. 1 Court, eventual champion Simona Halep ended the memorable run of then 15-year-old Coco Gauff with a 6-3, 6-3 victory. There were also typically dominant victories for Serena Williams and Novak Djokovic over Carla Suarez Navarro and Ugo Humbert respectively. However, things did not go to plan for top seed Ashleigh Barty on the No.2 Court, as the was defeated in three sets by Alison Riske, before third seed Karolina Pliskova followed her out after losing a gruelling match 4-6, 7-5, 13-11 to Czech compatriot Karolina Muchova

programme.title: Today at Wimbledon
programme.desc: Clare Balding introduces highlights of the seventh day's play at the All England Club, where the line-up for the quarter-finals in the singles draws was set to be decided. This is invariably one of the most exciting days on the schedule, with all of the men's and ladies' fourth-round singles matches set to be played

programme.title: Heist: The Northern Bank Robbery
programme.desc: Darragh MacIntyre and Sam McBride shed new light on the Northern Bank Robbery. Two families were held hostage for 24 hours, while two bank employees were forced to rob £26.5million. Despite an international police investigation and heavily rumoured IRA involvement, the case remains unsolved after 16 years. Darragh and Sam examine internal bank CCTV, police 999 calls, archive footage and court transcripts for fresh clues, and consider the impact of the crime on the peace process

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: A Dangerous Dynasty: House of Assad
programme.desc: Documentary about the al-Assad family, which has ruled Syria since 1971 and established an authoritarian government in the country under the control of the Ba'ath Party. Current Syrian president Bashar al-Assad trained to be an eye doctor at St Mary's Hospital in Paddington, London, and his wife Asma was born in Britain to a Syrian family. Told through rarely seen footage and testimony of those who knew and worked with them, the series explores how the couple, who at first were seen as a modernising force, ended up running a regime accused of war crimes

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Adam Henson and Charlotte Smith present the show from the Cumbrian Hills, and catch up with a farming mother and son who are diversifying to make ends meet. Charlotte also reports on efforts to protect the surrounding landscape. Plus, Joe Crowley investigates a worrying rise in attacks on livestock by dogs off the leash

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: The agents attempt to round up six horses that have been abandoned on an unsuspecting family's field. Ben and Myles meet another Sheriff as they arrive at a carpet showroom

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: Rav Wilding reveals how a team of 'super recognisers' - police officers with an extraordinary ability to recognise faces - could be the key to catching criminals who commit multiple offences

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine a case from 1882 of a wife accused of poisoning her husband's rice pudding with enough arsenic to kill 50 people

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp back at some gadgetry buys of the past, dropping in on some of the show's favourite experts. Anita Manning gets to learn all about the speaking clock, while Christina Trevanion gets the lowdown on barometer restoration

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost offers tips on laying turf, and planting succulents. He also adds perennial plants to his kitchen garden to grow as cut flowers. Plus, Joe Swift travels to Marwood Hill Gardens in Devon to explore plants that thrive in moist conditions

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson is back in her Inverleith Park allotment, where she is setting up a wormery and looking forward to a pretty and productive harvest. Carole Baxter and Mairi Rattray show how to prune and tame overgrown evergreens, and the Beechgrowers return with further updates in their gardening video diaries from the length and breadth of Scotland

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day eight of the tournament at the All England Club, where the women's singles quarter-finals are scheduled to be played, as well as the last eight of the men's doubles, and mixed doubles contests. Elina Svitolina recorded a 7-5, 6-4 victory over Karolina Muchova at this stage two years ago to reach the semi-finals of a Grand Slam for the first time in her career. Meanwhile, there were two victories in a day on Centre Court for Serena Williams, as she defeated Alison Riske 6-4, 4-6, 6-3, before returning later to team up with Andy Murray for a 7-5, 6-3 victory over Fabrice Martin and Raquel Atawo. In the men's doubles, Nicolas Mahut and Edouard Roger-Vasselin overcame top seeds Lukasz Kubot and Marcelo Melo in four sets

programme.title: Today at Wimbledon
programme.desc: Clare Balding introduces highlights of the eighth day's play at the All England Club, where the line-up for the ladies' singles semi-finals was due to be decided, as the battle to succeed Simona Halep as champion continued. Plus, there is a round-up of the day's headlines, as well as a look ahead to the men's singles quarter-finals, which are scheduled to enthral the crowds at SW19 tomorrow

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: Responding to a call of a brutal attack in a residential area of Dudley, West Midlands Police arrive on the scene to find the body of a young man. Soon afterwards, a second man, brought to hospital from the crime scene, is declared dead. Forensic Investigators must now piece together the evidence in this complex murder inquiry

programme.title: Cunk on Britain
programme.desc: Philomena Cunk from Charlie Brooker's Weekly Wipe carries out in-depth investigations, travelling the length and breadth of the country and enlisting the help of a variety of experts. She begins by examining the origins of the universe, and interviews Robert Peston about British politics. Comedy documentary, starring Diane Morgan

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: A Dangerous Dynasty: House of Assad
programme.desc: Bashar al-Assad is now president and his British-born wife Asma is First Lady, but they face problems on all sides and their regime is accused of killing a political enemy. Asma's in-laws don't like the idea of her being First Lady and want to keep her out of the limelight, an angry US president names Syria as part of the Axis of Evil and the United Nations issue resolutions that Bashar's family want him to ignore. The Assads soon find themselves at the centre of a murder investigation, as one of their political enemies is blown up in a car bombing

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works in construction, finding out what it takes to join the men and women who are building Britain. Around 2.4 million people work in the construction industry, making it one of the largest employers in the UK - worth over £100billion pounds in 2019 alone

programme.title: Brian Cox's Adventures in Space and Time
programme.desc: The physicist reflects on the most challenging and intriguing questions facing science today, using material from his past programmes and the latest scientific research. He begins by looking at how far the exploration of the cosmos can go, believing that humanity is at the start of a new age of travel where space flight is on the verge of becoming routine

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: Footage from a police body camera helps convict a violent offender who attacked a policewoman and the owners of a wine bar use facial recognition cameras to tackle pickpocketing

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine a notorious case of supposed matricide dating from the 1920s, which may have resulted in an innocent man being hanged

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charles Ross presents the show from Brackley, Northamptonshire, and discovers how old food can be collectable. With experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Vilma Jackson celebrates black deaf people who have achieved extraordinary things in the UK. Plus, meeting the creator of Flow TV. With voiceover

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including St Helens v Wigan Warriors and Warrington Wolves v Leeds Rhinos

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day nine of the tournament at the All England Club, where the men's singles quarter-finals are scheduled to be played, as well as the ladies' doubles quarter-finals, and mixed doubles matches. Spaniards Roberto Bautista Agut and Rafael Nadal both booked semi-final spots at this stage two years ago on No. 1 Court, defeating Argentina's Guido Pella and American Sam Querrey respectively. Meanwhile, in the mixed doubles competition, top seeds Bruno Soares and Nicole Melica were able to overcome the star power of Andy Murray and Serena Williams, prevailing 6-4, 4-6, 6-2, and second ladies' doubles seeds Barbora Krejcikova and Katerina Siniakova progressed in straight sets against Anna-Lena Gronefeld and Demi Schuurs

programme.title: Today at Wimbledon
programme.desc: Clare Balding introduces highlights of day nine's play at the All England Club, where the line-up for the men's singles semi-finals was due to be decided. In addition to detailed analysis of those contests, there is a round-up of the latest headlines, as well as a look ahead to the ladies' singles semi-finals, which are scheduled for Centre Court tomorrow

programme.title: Hampton Court Palace Garden Festival
programme.desc: Joe Swift, Rachel de Thame, Adam Frost and Arit Anderson head up the coverage as the world's largest annual flower show reopens for 2021. There is a range of diverse garden designs to explore, including a lifestyle category, global impact gardens addressing environmental challenges and large show-stopping gardens and features. Also, for the first time, the presenters pair up to create a themed Hampton flower border their very own competition. With analysis and advice from Carol Klein, Toby Buckland, Nick Bailey and Mark Lane

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Josh Widdicombe, Susan Calman and Rich Hall join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig for an unconventional look at the theme 'Origins and Openings'. During the course of the show, the panellists find out an assortment of facts, including what Mr First thought of Mr Second. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window, as the fiendishly difficult questions lead to points being awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: A Dangerous Dynasty: House of Assad
programme.desc: As a glamorous profile of Asma appears in Vogue magazine, the Arab Spring protests reach Syria. Bashar must decide whether to concede to their demands for greater freedom or follow in his father Hafez's famously hardline steps. What unfolds shocks the world in its brutality, but as the chaos of civil war rages, the so-called Islamic State emerges and it looks like Bashar is on the brink of losing the Assad dynasty's control of Syria

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Vilma Jackson celebrates black deaf people who have achieved extraordinary things in the UK. Plus, meeting the creator of Flow TV. With voiceover

programme.title: Building Britain's Biggest Nuclear Power Station
programme.desc: Part one of two. Following the challenges involved with building the Hinkley Point C in the remote Somerset countryside, the first new nuclear power station in Britain for a generation. This series follows the engineers, technicians, and the behind-the-scenes staff who are under pressure to keep the project on track, including building the mammoth foundations for the two nuclear reactors, and excavating cooling water tunnels out under the Bristol Channel

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: As news of the Covid-19 virus broke around the world, teams of scientists jumped into action to create a vaccine against a virus that no one had heard of before. This programme looks at the extraordinary inside story of the unprecedented quest to develop vaccines to fight Covid-19, with unique access to five teams of scientists around the globe. Narrated by Lesley Sharp

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a night scene, with waves crashing against rocks and a lighthouse

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Jack and Michelle have an encounter with a school of Mexican dolphins, while casino workers Dale and Mary swap the roulette wheel for rollerblades, sending everyone running for cover

programme.title: Art of Persia
programme.desc: Broadcaster and journalist Samira Ahmed reveals how Iran preserved its distinctive language and culture despite the Arab conquest of Persia

programme.title: Turner: The Man Who Painted Britain
programme.desc: Tim Marlow presents this documentary exploring the achievement of JMW Turner, revealing how the artist's revolutionary works changed the course of painting forever. The programme chronicles the highs and lows of Turner's life, including his tragic childhood, secret affairs and the exhausting extremes to which he pushed himself in order to capture a unique quality in his paintings

programme.title: Simon Schama's Power of Art
programme.desc: Turner's painting The Slave Ship, which courted controversy in its portrayal of slave traders throwing their dying cargo into the sea to claim the insurance money. The work demonstrates the artist's move from traditional styles to a determination to test the limits of both technique and acceptable subject matter

programme.title: What Do Artists Do All Day?
programme.desc: Internationally renowned abstract artist Frank Bowling became the first black Royal Academician in 2005. As a major retrospective opens at Tate Britain, Brenda Emmanus meets him to look back over his long career and there are interviews with critics and fellow artists to discuss the significance of his work in the history of British art

programme.title: Apples, Pears and Paint: How to Make a Still Life Painting
programme.desc: Examining the history of the art form that depicts fruit, flowers and domestic objects, from the Xenia murals at Pompeii through to the cubist works of Picasso. The film also takes a look at the work of some of the artists synonymous with the genre, including Chardin and Cezanne, and features contributions from Bettany Hughes, Janina Ramirez and Andrew Graham-Dixon

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a night scene, with waves crashing against rocks and a lighthouse

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Jack and Michelle have an encounter with a school of Mexican dolphins, while casino workers Dale and Mary swap the roulette wheel for rollerblades, sending everyone running for cover

programme.title: Turner: The Man Who Painted Britain
programme.desc: Tim Marlow presents this documentary exploring the achievement of JMW Turner, revealing how the artist's revolutionary works changed the course of painting forever. The programme chronicles the highs and lows of Turner's life, including his tragic childhood, secret affairs and the exhausting extremes to which he pushed himself in order to capture a unique quality in his paintings

programme.title: Art of Persia
programme.desc: Broadcaster and journalist Samira Ahmed reveals how Iran preserved its distinctive language and culture despite the Arab conquest of Persia

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross creates a painting of a large old mill in the heart of a forest

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: The performers are distressed to learn they have a mere three days to stage a new musical extravaganza, and so they should be _ the props won't work and lead singer Michelle has lost her voice. On shore, Mary and Dale encounter thousands of turtles and Jane is surprised to find a well-known high-street store on a tropical island

programme.title: Yes Minister
programme.desc: A change in administration policy leaves Jim lumbered with the unenviable task of shaking up local government bureaucracy. Political comedy, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: Tom and Barbara introduce livestock into Surbiton - an idea that does not go down too well with the neighbours. Comedy, starring Richard Briers and Felicity Kendal

programme.title: Hemingway
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of Ernest Hemingway, considered one of the greatest American writers. This instalment revisits the publication of Our Time and The Sun Also Rises, and the later critical and commercial success he enjoyed with his second novel, A Farewell to Arms. Narrated by Peter Coyote

programme.title: Michael Palin's Hemingway Adventure
programme.desc: The journey progresses from the author's birthplace in the American Midwest to northern Michigan, where the intrepid adventurer spends a few hours trying his hand at fishing and shooting _ as Hemingway did as a child _ before following the spirited young man's path across the Atlantic into Europe

programme.title: Philly DA: Breaking the Law
programme.desc: Formerly incarcerated activist LaTonya Myers begins work at the public defenders' office, while dealing with a stressful new probation sentence of nearly a decade. Going into his second year, Krasner makes reforming probation a major goal, but he must win over the judges to make this happen

programme.title: Art of America
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon concludes his journey through American art by examining how it reflected and challenged the country's post-war culture. He begins in the purpose-built suburb of Levittown, Pennsylvania, before travelling to New York to examine works by Jasper Johns and Andy Warhol, then revealing how the otherworldly qualities of Los Angeles inspired painter Ed Ruscha. The presenter also discusses his love for the work of Philip Guston, meets Warhol's former lover Billy Name and artists Jeff Koons and James Rosenquist, and assesses artistic responses to the September 11, 2001 terrorist attacks

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross creates a painting of a large old mill in the heart of a forest

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: The performers are distressed to learn they have a mere three days to stage a new musical extravaganza, and so they should be _ the props won't work and lead singer Michelle has lost her voice. On shore, Mary and Dale encounter thousands of turtles and Jane is surprised to find a well-known high-street store on a tropical island

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Amanda's PTA charity fundraiser is doubling up as her birthday celebration, meaning everyone is dragged into the big event. However, the mix of sponsored cycling with Anne's lethal cocktails results in a night of high drama and big revelations. Meanwhile, Kevin's attempts to clear the air with Amanda just make everything worse, and a put-upon Anne finally loses her temper

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: As an outbreak of headlice sweeps the school, Julia finds herself accused of triggering a second wave. Ostracised by the other mums, she needs to find a way back into their good books and decides to throw a nit treatment party at her home. The gathering reveals that Anne has some big news, Meg is facing a crisis, Kevin has committed a terrible crime of passion and Liz is waiting to hear about a possible career move. Comedy, starring Anna Maxwell Martin and Diane Morgan

programme.title: Hemingway
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of Ernest Hemingway, considered one of the greatest American writers. This instalment revisits the publication of Our Time and The Sun Also Rises, and the later critical and commercial success he enjoyed with his second novel, A Farewell to Arms. Narrated by Peter Coyote

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene featuring the vibrant golden red glow of a campfire, complete with a cowboy resting by the crackling flames

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Jane treats herself to a makeover at the ship's hairdresser, while Dale and Mary enjoy a romantic rafting trip in Jamaica. The new on-board show gets ready to open - if Michelle's weakened voice can take the strain

programme.title: Japan: Earth's Enchanted Islands
programme.desc: Michelle Dockery narrates a documentary examining the wildlife of the east Asian country, beginning on the central island of Honshu. It is home to more than 100 million people, but most of it is mountainous and this wilderness is home to an astonishing range of wildlife, and people and nature are drawn together in the most unexpected ways

programme.title: The Joy of Winning
programme.desc: A witty, mind-expanding guide in which Hannah Fry unlocks the essential strategies a person needs to get what they want - to win more of the time. The mathematician also hails 20th-century scientists John von Neumann and John Nash, who worked out the science of success and reveals how the same rules apply to the world's biggest problems - whether its avoiding nuclear annihilation or tackling climate change

programme.title: Storyville

programme.title: Maddie, the Plants and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie on an adventure around the world of flowers to see how we can help to look after them and our planet. Find out how flowers and different animals, like bees, birds, butterflies and insects, work together. We learn how bees and insects pollinate flowers, as Maddie becomes a bee and plays the pollination game! Along the way, we’ll discover flowers of all different shapes, sizes and colours and learn why they are so different. We discover the different parts of a flower, the stigma, the anther, the stamen and the petals and how some flowers go to sleep at night and open in the day!

programme.desc: Join Maddie on a journey of discovery to find out about plants around the world. Find out how we can help to look after them and our planet, and why and how they grow and survive in different climates. Maddie explores plants in the rainforest, big and small, and sees how the clever drip-tip leaves work watering the plants beneath them. We’ll see how plants in hot, dry parts of world like the desert thrive in their own special ways, like the cactus plant that stores litres of water in its waxy leaves. And we’ll learn how plants that grow close to home in the UK, can grow in a colder and wetter climate.

programme.desc: Join Maddie to discover the plants that we use in our everyday lives and how we can help to look after them and our planet. programme.desc: Explore a puzzle of a lemur, colourful tile-matching blocks and Book Girl making a plane.

programme.title: Let's Play
programme.desc: Rebecca has her work cut out when she steps in to help Mr Bucket, the local plumber, who is tied up on another job

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Bobble is playing at being invisible, but everyone can see him

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Grandmaster Glitch turns Segovia Aqueduct into a giant waterslide and the Go Jetters must stop it toppling to the ground. Cartoon following four superheroes

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: When Andy breaks a museum artefact, he travels 30 million years back in time to replace it, where he tracks down a chalicothere and gets chased by a swarm of prehistoric hornets

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: The Brooklyn-Bells decorate Doug's bedroom. Waffle tries to help but causes a green paint disaster and escapes into Mrs Hobbs' house next door

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat
programme.desc: Jack and Salty are lost in the Antarctic, and only one friend can save them - the little penguin

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Dad takes Tig to the swing park and she sees her friend playing with children she does not know. The little girl feels nervous, but Woolly gives her the confidence to join them

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘feather’, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see lots of different types of feathers – a grey feather, a spotty feather, a fluffy feather and a unicorn feather. There’s a help-you-fly feather, some fluffy feathers and even a drawing feather! The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word!

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: B, O and X discover a dressing-up box with everything they need to put on a performance of Sleeping Beauty

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Messy gets to fly Mayor Oki's plane, but this is no easy jet and he crash lands in a tree. Luckily, robots fly to the rescue. Science-educational series following a curious, adventure-seeking and unruly monster

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects associated with the colour orange, from oranges to orangutans, and explore the deeper meaning of the colour. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour orange.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friend become animal keepers at an animal park. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble looks after an animal too - his friend, Spotty the horse

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The friends play with a bubble and then Po and Laa-Laa play catch with a ball. In Tummy Tales, two brothers learn to catch a ball and play catch with their dog

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny gets stuck in Padget's shop while playing a game of hide-and-seek with Flop

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Someone stops the samba at the Rio Carnival. Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Timmy hears thunder and tries to make loud noises himself

programme.title: Footy Pups
programme.desc: Pickles' wobbly dribbling helps the Footy Pups in a game against Moleton Wonderers. Meanwhile, Arsenal player Rachel Yankey helps youngsters practise their dribbling skills

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Cook and Line discover the Pirate Handbook says all crews should vote for their own captain, and put themselves forward to replace Captain Captain

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The dog blows a lot of bubbles with his pot of bubble mixture. Though it is quite hard to do, most of the squirrels eventually get the hang of it and the clubhouse rapidly fills up with lots of bubbles

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Peter learns not to judge a book by its cover when he takes an elderly tortoise for a quiet walk in the woods. Animated tale inspired by Beatrix Potter

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: The Potatoes go on an imaginary hippie journey and sing about the amazing things a person can do when they give their imagination a try

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie sees how prosthetic legs are made and meets a special dog that can detect nuts

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Kyan wants to climb Mount Everest, but there's a giant snowball heading his way - and it's got a moustache!

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Molly examines her shelf of special things at the back of Mack’s Stall. She chooses the joke squirty flower and remembers the day she got it. When Molly and Mack take a delivery of a big box of practical jokes, Alice reveals she loves playing practical jokes on people. Alice decides to play a prank on James, which the kids at Kids Club think is hilarious, especially Magnus. Soon, James is plotting his revenge and gets his own back on Alice. Before long, there’s a ‘prank-off’ underway. As the jokes continue, Magnus gets caught up in the excitement. But, when he decides to play his own prank, he accidentally takes it too far and upsets Suki. James and Alice feel bad that they let things get out of hand, and Magnus needs to find a way to make it up to his friend.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Animated fun for younger viewers featuring tales of the world's worst puppy who woofs and wags his way into the lives of an unassuming family

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: Gran Gran busts a groove when she teaches JoJo the moves to a traditional Saint Lucian dance. JoJo finds that learning a dance can take time, until she realises she can put her own twist on it.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The feathered friend wins a trip for two to a rather exciting hotel, and finds that he settles in much quicker than Sarah

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: After discovering two paper crowns, Pepi Nana and Little Nana decide to be the Big Princess and the Little Princess. In Storyland the Little Princess goes to visit the Big Princess in her Big Castle, where she enjoys the biggest tea she has ever had

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Makka Pakka visits the garden to wash everyone's faces, including the tiny Pontipines

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: An exciting story read by a special guest. Dan Walker reads Ambrose Goes for Gold, by Tor Freeman.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin catches up with an ambitious project to create a forever home in Crewe. Plus, a former stables in Derby and a studio flat in London's Paddington

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine a notorious case of supposed matricide dating from the 1920s, which may have resulted in an innocent man being hanged

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, following the work of those providing emergency and medical care to the Shetland Islands' 23,000 residents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charles Ross presents the show from Brackley, Northamptonshire, and discovers how old food can be collectable. With experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day seven of the tournament at the All England Club, where fourth-round matches in the men's and women's singles are scheduled to be played. British number one Johanna Konta delighted the home crowd when she came from a set down to defeat two-time champion Petra Kvitova at this stage two years ago. She was followed on Centre Court by Roger Federer, who was in dominant form as he overwhelmed 17th seed Matteo Berrettini 6-1, 6-2, 6-2

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Iolo's Pembrokeshire
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores Pembrokeshire's stunning landscape and goes in search of its hidden wildlife spots, finding a rare butterfly and an old oil storage site where hundreds of orchids have bloomed. Iolo also walks round St David's Head, where he finds fearsome looking beetles and Britain's only venomous snake

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Frankie is feeling downbeat so Zack offers to take her on a driving lesson - but his good deed has terrible consequences. Kat asks Denise to reconsider letting Phil into Raymond's life, Patrick returns the things that Isaac stole to a shocked Ruby and Martin, and Peter resolves to help a heartbroken Bobby move on with his life

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Report on British businesses dealing with post-Brexit trading rules six months after the UK left the EU single market, trying to maintain relationships with their European customers while also seeking new markets further afield. The programme features personal accounts from fishermen in Scotland to entrepreneurs in the home counties

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how Britain turned its sporting reputation around over 16 years to triumph at the 2012 Olympics. The first episode begins with the UK's poor performance in the 1996 games at Atlanta, and how the Government diverted National Lottery funding to provide the best possible training for the nation's athletes. Featuring contributions from Linford Christie and Kelly Holmes

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: The case of Michaella McCollum, a teenager from Northern Ireland who formed a relationship with a drug trafficker during her first trip abroad in Ibiza. He persuaded her to assist him in smuggling drugs for his cartel contacts, and just a few weeks later, she was arrested at Lima airport in Peru with more than a million pounds worth of cocaine in her suitcase

programme.title: Cristiano Ronaldo: Impossible to Ignore
programme.desc: The story of Portuguese football star Cristiano Ronaldo's life and career, revealing how he became the world's biggest sporting brand

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor Stephen Mangan hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Janey Godley and journalist Helen Lewis joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day eight of the tournament at the All England Club, where the women's singles quarter-finals are scheduled to be played. British number one Johanna Konta missed out on a second semi-final in three years at this stage in 2019 when she was beaten 7-6, 6-1 by the Czech Republic's Barbora Strycova, while eventual champion Simona Halep recorded a victory by the exact same scoreline against Zhang Shuai

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Italy v Spain (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the opening semi-final, as the winners of the second and first quarter-finals meet at Wembley Stadium in a repeat of the 2012 final. The Azzurri, led by former Manchester City boss Roberto Mancini, have looked strong throughout the tournament. They breezed through the group stage with maximum points and no goals conceded, and have overcome Austria and Belgium by consecutive 2-1 scorelines to reach the last four. Spain's route to the semis has been a rockier one, with Luis Enrique's side finishing second in Group E, and winning a remarkable last-16 game with Croatia by a 5-3 scoreline. They then needed a penalty shootout to overcome Switzerland in the quarter-finals, but all of that will be forgotten ahead of this clash with another of Europe's most successful nations

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Peter Crouch, Maya Jama and Alex Horne are back for more live Euros fun and the stakes are high as the first of the semi-final gets under way. Tonight they'll be joined by stars of People Just Do Nothing, Kurupt FM, for the usual mix of chat and games. Plus, there will be live music by the Horne Section

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Italy v Spain. Action from the opening semi-final, as the winners of the second and first quarter-finals met at Wembley Stadium

programme.desc: Norma and Norman attempt to reconnect as they plan for the future, while Dylan and Romero decide to take action for Norman's own good

programme.desc: Norman tries to put together a special event for Norma, and Romero is confronted with the consequences of his actions

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day nine of the tournament at the All England Club, where the men's singles quarter-finals are scheduled to be played. Novak Djokovic and Roger Federer demonstrated why they were still the top two seeds after many years at the pinnacle of the game when they recorded victories over David Goffin and Kei Nishikori respectively at this stage in 2019, and the two eventually met in the final, with Djokovic prevailing

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Coverage of the second semi-final (Kick-off 8.00pm), as the winners of the fourth and third quarter-finals meet at Wembley Stadium. A brace by Antoine Griezmann put hosts France in the final at the expense of Germany at this stage of the 2016 tournament, with Didier Deschamps' men proving to be the more clinical and claim a fairly comfortable victory despite the Germans having 65% of the possession. The sides also met in the group stage of this tournament, with the French again prevailing, this time 1-0 courtesy of Mats Hummels' own goal

programme.desc: Peter Crouch, Maya Jama and Alex Horne are joined by former England star Alex Scott and comedian Jack Whitehall for the last of their live shows, as they react to the concluding semi-final and look forward to the final

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Frankie is feeling downbeat so Zack offers to take her on a driving lesson - but his good deed has terrible consequences. Kat asks Denise to reconsider letting Phil into Raymond's life, Patrick returns the things that Isaac stole to a shocked Ruby and Martin, and Peter resolves to help a heartbroken Bobby move on with his life

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Report on British businesses dealing with post-Brexit trading rules six months after the UK left the EU single market, trying to maintain relationships with their European customers while also seeking new markets further afield. The programme features personal accounts from fishermen in Scotland to entrepreneurs in the home counties

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how Britain turned its sporting reputation around over 16 years to triumph at the 2012 Olympics. The first episode begins with the UK's poor performance in the 1996 games at Atlanta, and how the Government diverted National Lottery funding to provide the best possible training for the nation's athletes. Featuring contributions from Linford Christie and Kelly Holmes

programme.title: Countryside College
programme.desc: The summer term sees the nation in lockdown due to the pandemic. The CAFRE students continue their studies online and many have become essential workers. Final year agricultural student Jack Nevin hoped to graduate in June, but his college days have been cut short and he finds himself back working on the family dairy farm outside Portrush

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: The case of Michaella McCollum, a teenager from Northern Ireland who formed a relationship with a drug trafficker during her first trip abroad in Ibiza. He persuaded her to assist him in smuggling drugs for his cartel contacts, and just a few weeks later, she was arrested at Lima airport in Peru with more than a million pounds worth of cocaine in her suitcase

programme.title: Cristiano Ronaldo: Impossible to Ignore
programme.desc: The story of Portuguese football star Cristiano Ronaldo's life and career, revealing how he became the world's biggest sporting brand

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor Stephen Mangan hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Janey Godley and journalist Helen Lewis joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a Victorian semi in London's Forest Hill, a terraced house in Stoke-on-Trent, and a piece of land in Staffordshire

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate an infamous Scottish murder case from 1862 where a compelling alternative suspect could have been the true killer

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, following the work of those providing emergency and medical care to the Shetland Islands' 23,000 residents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and John Cameron help the red and blue teams search for valuable items in Leominster, Herefordshire, in the hope of making a profit at auction. Plus, host Anita Manning finds out more about Worcester's saucy secret

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day eight of the tournament at the All England Club, where the women's singles quarter-finals are scheduled to be played. British number one Johanna Konta missed out on a second semi-final in three years at this stage in 2019 when she was beaten 7-6, 6-1 by the Czech Republic's Barbora Strycova, while eventual champion Simona Halep recorded a victory by the exact same scoreline against Zhang Shuai

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Italy v Spain (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the opening semi-final, as the winners of the second and first quarter-finals meet at Wembley Stadium in a repeat of the 2012 final. The Azzurri, led by former Manchester City boss Roberto Mancini, have looked strong throughout the tournament. They breezed through the group stage with maximum points and no goals conceded, and have overcome Austria and Belgium by consecutive 2-1 scorelines to reach the last four. Spain's route to the semis has been a rockier one, with Luis Enrique's side finishing second in Group E, and winning a remarkable last-16 game with Croatia by a 5-3 scoreline. They then needed a penalty shootout to overcome Switzerland in the quarter-finals, but all of that will be forgotten ahead of this clash with another of Europe's most successful nations

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Peter Crouch, Maya Jama and Alex Horne are back for more live Euros fun and the stakes are high as the first of the semi-final gets under way. Tonight they'll be joined by stars of People Just Do Nothing, Kurupt FM, for the usual mix of chat and games. Plus, there will be live music by the Horne Section

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Italy v Spain. Action from the opening semi-final, as the winners of the second and first quarter-finals met at Wembley Stadium

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma and Norman attempt to reconnect as they plan for the future, while Dylan and Romero decide to take action for Norman's own good

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman tries to put together a special event for Norma, and Romero is confronted with the consequences of his actions

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Italy v Spain. A chance to see the opening semi-final at Wembley Stadium, as the sides contested a place in the showpiece match

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of a former commercial unit in Madeley in Staffordshire, a fire-damaged house in Wolverhampton and a former care home in Ilkeston in Derbyshire

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein explore the controversial killing of a police officer which sparked the round-up of dozens of suspects and ended with three men going to the gallows

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Charles Hanson help two teams look for items at Oswestry Showground in Shropshire, with presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp takes a trip along Pontcysyllte aqueduct

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day nine of the tournament at the All England Club, featuring action from the outside courts

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day nine of the tournament at the All England Club, where the men's singles quarter-finals are scheduled to be played. Novak Djokovic and Roger Federer demonstrated why they were still the top two seeds after many years at the pinnacle of the game when they recorded victories over David Goffin and Kei Nishikori respectively at this stage in 2019, and the two eventually met in the final, with Djokovic prevailing

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Coverage of the second semi-final (Kick-off 8.00pm), as the winners of the fourth and third quarter-finals meet at Wembley Stadium. A brace by Antoine Griezmann put hosts France in the final at the expense of Germany at this stage of the 2016 tournament, with Didier Deschamps' men proving to be the more clinical and claim a fairly comfortable victory despite the Germans having 65% of the possession. The sides also met in the group stage of this tournament, with the French again prevailing, this time 1-0 courtesy of Mats Hummels' own goal

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Peter Crouch, Maya Jama and Alex Horne are joined by former England star Alex Scott and comedian Jack Whitehall for the last of their live shows, as they react to the concluding semi-final and look forward to the final

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: England v Denmark. Action from the second semi-final, as the winners of the fourth and third quarter-finals met at Wembley Stadium

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how Britain turned its sporting reputation around over 16 years to triumph at the 2012 Olympics. The first episode begins with the UK's poor performance in the 1996 games at Atlanta, and how the Government diverted National Lottery funding to provide the best possible training for the nation's athletes. Featuring contributions from Linford Christie and Kelly Holmes

programme.desc: A chance to see the second semi-final at Wembley Stadium, as the line-up for Sunday's showpiece match was confirmed

programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Su Pic (Peek Zoo)
programme.desc: It’s a special day at Sù Pìc - the day when visitors come to watch the flamingos dancing together

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The little rabbit is not feeling very well, but Sula has a surprise that cheers him up

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing goes to Padget's shop as he has enough money to buy one toy

programme.title: 'S E Iasg a Th'Annam (I'm a Fish)
programme.desc: The Koi tells us how they are kept as domesticated fish in ponds and how you can tell what age they are by looking at their scales

programme.title: Òrain CBeebies
programme.desc: A selection of songs from Carrie, Linda, Ruairidh and the CBeebies ALBA presenters

programme.title: Leum is Danns (Jump and Dance)
programme.desc: Dancing and jumping fun for pre-school children

programme.title: Su Shiusaidh (Little Suzy's Zoo)
programme.desc: With a blueberry stain marking the back of his head, Witzy is looking rather scary and manages to frighten all his friends

programme.title: Rita is Crogall
programme.desc: Rita and Crocodile go for a walk in the forest to collect as many conkers as they can

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Daibhidh Walker reads Bi air d’ fhaiceall ron Chrogall, written by Martin Jenkins, illustrated by Satoshi Kitamura and translated into Gaelic by Jo Macdonald

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda
programme.desc: When Po accidentally wrecks the training room, he goes in search of help in order to repair the damage before Shifu realises what has happened. However, he inadvertently ends up inviting an old enemy into the Palace!

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Bouncing on the trampoline is the latest activity

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Fun science experiments, including making clouds in a jar and how to find out which bouncing ball is spinning the fastest. Presented by Kerry Anne MacLeod and Allan MacDonald

programme.title: Puirt-adhair (Highland Airports)
programme.desc: At the Highland Gliding Club, 14-year-old Francesca Mountain gets to do her first ever loop the loop. The night of the annual 5K Midnight Runway run at Inverness Airport sees Callum Smith and Dan Mason kept on their toes

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: A look back at 'Bath day' for soldiers of the First World War

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: Stories of life during lockdown, including a South Uist-based nurse who is involved in the vaccination roll out and a Stornoway-based taxi driver whose livelihood has been impacted

programme.title: Gothenburg '83
programme.desc: Former Manchester United manager Alex Ferguson recalls how he led Aberdeen to glory in the 1983 European Cup Winners' Cup

programme.title: Mach à Seo!
programme.desc: Ramsay MacMahon heads to the Orkney Islands - an archipelago of scenic fertile islands and turquoise blue waters - where he has a busy few days exploring

programme.title: Julie Fowlis: Ceol Aig Baile
programme.desc: Filmed during lockdown, Julie Fowlis invites musicians from around the world to perform from their own homes. This episode features Xabier Diaz, Guadi Galego and Karen Matheson

programme.title: Bruadar a' Bhàis (A Dream of Death)
programme.desc: When a young man was convicted of murder in Inverness in 1830, it was the only time in Scots legal history that testimony from second sight was admitted in court. This dramatised documentary revisits the crime and subsequent trial

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.desc: David and the team are in Milton Keynes, where a seller makes a thousand percent profit from Tracy Thackeray-Howitt and Mark Stevens gets some gold to really smile about

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Warwick Davis hosts the quiz, in which a team of five mobile fish and chip shop workers answers questions about top 10 lists then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Sarah, Jon, Becca and Steve pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Coronation Street

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by chef Galton Blackiston for a tour of Cornwall, sampling some world-class ice cream and cooking fresh seafood tapas with chipotle dressing. The trip concludes with a visit to Sennen beach, where they practice their oil painting and break out the barbecue

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell return to tell incredible stories of people desperate to find missing family and answer questions that have haunted entire lives. This week features two stories of children searching for their birth mothers after life-changing events - former Scotland footballer Dominic Matteo, who started to search after suffering a life-threatening brain tumour, and a woman, pained with emotion on her wedding day because her birth mother was missing

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Extraordinary Twins
programme.desc: Part two of two. Three-year-olds Callie and Carter are approaching their fourth birthday, and parents Nick and Chelsea are under pressure to make a decision about whether to keep them joined together or put them through surgery to separate them. They meet other conjoined twins to help them decide

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: The first leg of The Tour of France sees Ali, Dovey and Spooner head to the south of the country near Marseille, to a large natural lake called Carces. This tricky wild water, situated within rocky terrain, is rumoured by locals to hold several carp over the magical 70lb mark. This adventure is far from plain sailing as the team are faced with some huge physical and mental challenges - extreme weather conditions, heavy weed beds and giant catfish

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Highlights from the first week of this year's race, as the rider's took a well-deserved rest day. Gary Imlach, Ned Boulting, David Millar and Daniel Friebe look back on the racing so far and preview what is still to come. Plus, the team will be at the team press conferences to speak to the contenders

programme.title: Lingo

programme.title: Tenable

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Adam, Nathan, Emma and Rita work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: England v Denmark (Kick-off 8.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the second semi-final, as the winners of the fourth and third quarter-finals meet at Wembley Stadium. The Three Lions have grown into this tournament, topping Group D thanks to efficient if not spectacular 1-0 wins over Croatia and Czech Republic, and a 0-0 draw with Scotland. They then laid to rest some of their past demons with a 2-0 victory over Germany in the last-16, and thrashed Ukraine 4-0 in Rome in the quarter-finals, becoming the first team in the history of the competition to keep five consecutive clean sheets in their first five games. Denmark have bounced back from the brink of elimination to reach the last four, riding a wave of emotion along the way. Influential midfielder Christian Eriksen collapsed on the pitch and suffered a cardiac arrest during their opening match with Finland, which they would go on to lose 1-0. They were then beaten 2-1 by Belgium in their second group match, but came storming

programme.desc: When a member of Oxford University's examinations board is found dead, Morse deduces he must have overheard a conversation someone wanted to keep secret at all costs. However, his theory has one major flaw - the victim was deaf. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Barbara Flynn, Michael Gough, Clive Swift, Roger Lloyd Pack and Frederick Treves

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: As the day of his wedding finally arrives, Nick decides to avoid the hurly-burly of the police house by taking a soothing drive in the country - where he promptly gets involved in a domestic dispute and ends up stranded on the moors miles from anywhere
