programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: For the third visit of the week, the group heads to the Sleeperdorm Backpacker Hotel in Newcastle, where Ian Nicholls hopes his guests will enjoy his cheap and cheerful concept

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B battle continues at the Parsonage Hotel and Spa near York, a 55-room country manor which wows the guests with its size, although Mandi's high cleaning standards are not met

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day of the competition sees the B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. Oaklea Barns is first, and hosts Mandi and Paul want answers from Mark and Natalie, which ends up fanning flames

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Chris Moller takes a tour of three exceptional Grand Designs homes from the last four seasons to see how they've developed and to ask the big question - was it all worth it?

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Cavachon Stampy has been hit by a car and is in a critical condition, leaving owners Jim and his wife Karen facing a difficult dilemma - to take the chance and put their pet through painful surgery, or let him die. Golden retriever Dylan has a limp, and owner Stuart has driven seven hours from Preston to seek Noel Fitzpatrick's help. Meanwhile, Ginny from Chelsea arrives with her beloved dachshund Lilly-Belle, who has lost the use of her back legs

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge celebrates his birthday with a hot-air balloon ride over the chateau at sunrise, before setting to work in the kitchen making his own jam and alcohol from the vast quantities of fresh fruit waiting to be harvested in the garden. Angel Adoree starts work on a new project of her own - building a geodesic dome on top of a floating platform

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Jonathan Broom and his wife Deborah have put everything on hold while he pursues his dream of building a miniature Hollywood Hills-style mansion. They stumble across a scrap of land in north London, but it's fraught with problems and the only way they can complete the £1million home is by sinking half of it six metres below ground. Surrounded on all sides by their neighbours' back gardens, the couple have to dismantle walls and fences and promise to put them back together again, and that's just the start of their difficulties. Kevin McCloud follows their progress

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets energy conservationist Andrew, who has decided to build a radical, self-heating home for his family - the first of its kind in the UK. He aims to heat the house all year round by storing the warmth of the summer sun into insulated earth banks over and around the house, and then drawing the warmth back into the living spaces in winter. However, this pioneering construction seems an exercise in trial and error from the start, and what began as a seemingly simple, pure concept becomes a complicated feat of engineering

programme.title: The Billion Pound Hotel
programme.desc: Shaped like a giant sail and standing more than 1,000ft tall, the Burj Al Arab in Dubai cost an estimated £1billion to build, and has been dubbed the world's most luxurious hotel. Guests paying anything up to £10,000 a night expect only the highest of standards, so what does it take for the staff to satisfy these demanding customers? This programme follows the stories of a variety of visitors, from the King of Uganda to a firefighter who has saved up months of wages, and the workers who serve them

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 74-year-old man is brought in after being found in a confused state by his son. Doctors discover signs of a brain injury, leading his children to reveal the tragedy that befell their father and the shattering effect it has had on his life. A woman who fell down the stairs at her granddaughter's 16th birthday is also in need of treatment, and her daughter is reminded of a phone call that changed their lives forever. Meanwhile, in paediatrics, a talkative nine-year-old is treated for a rash on his ankle

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, with team captain Sean Lock and Johnny Vegas taking on guest captain Alan Carr and Lou Sanders. Lexicographer Susie Dent is joined by the Brett Domino Trio to adjudicate in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

Plus, Kate Quilton looks into the surprising difference between runny and set honey

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp introduces new crafts, skills and techniques for interior decoration. In this edition, she reveals how to create a personalised doormat and coasters, using spray paint, old tiles and serviettes

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate officers search for the heirs of an unclaimed estate, and reveal the story of a woman who left her native Ireland in the 1950s and was not heard of again until she died leaving a legacy of £1.7million. Plus, more details of unclaimed assets

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien search for more gems hidden among unwanted possessions, trying to make a profit from church organ pipes and old leather sports car seats

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole calls in a locksmith to bring a safe back from the scrapheap, and a ship's wheel is left in splinters when the presenter falls out with restorer Guy

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and mechanic Fuzz Townshend head to West Sussex to rescue an iconic 1970s motor - and the first truly successful Japanese car export - the mighty Datsun 240z

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Medical staff use battlefield dressings to try to save the life of a man who's bleeding to death. Nine-year-old Ar-Rayyan has been hit by a car and has a nasty head injury. And a fall leaves septuagenarian Bonnie with a severely fractured ankle

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Causilgey Manor in Truro, Cornwall, where former art dealers Robin and Brenda Spence want to treat their guests to a stay in the countryside

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Elmfield B&B in Devon, where retired politician Martin Perry and housekeeper Tracey Barrett pride themselves on keeping things traditional

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at the Chocolate Box in Bournemouth, where the hosts playful approach leaves their guests questioning their business credentials

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to the Buckle Seaford B&B in East Sussex, where an afternoon of football confirms the women's suspicions about hosts Julie and Jason Martin's competitive streak

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time, and Causilgey Manor owners Robin and Brenda want to get to grips with Julie and Jason's gripes over privacy

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Joseph and Susannah battle storms and complex topography as they attempt to build a coastal paradise on a steep site with stunning view of Piha

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A poodle called Diamond has been referred for a total hip replacement, and another, with the same owner, needs a new knee. Noel Fitzpatrick operates on both dogs on the same day, and doesn't finish until 3am. Hopity, a 12-week-old Balinese kitten, has a deformity on one of her back legs, which she could end up losing, while the operation itself is a huge risk. Hazell has brought in Dandi, a five month-old German shepherd who is lame in both front legs

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: As Dick and Angel prepare for winter, they attend a food festival in Rouen, where they hope to find inspiration over what to do with their fresh fruit and vegetables. Angel adds the finishing touches to the floating geodesic dome in time for a family sleepover, while Dick gets to work fixing the crumbling stucco

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Commercial architect Martin Walker and his art-director wife Kae want to build the ultimate family home near York, inspired by a giant farm shed. However, her idea for a warm and cosy design conflicts dramatically with his penchant for building large square warehouses, and finding something they both agree on proves extremely challenging on their tight budget. Kevin McCloud follows the couple's progress

programme.title: The Windsors: Inside the Royal Dynasty
programme.desc: This episode focuses on the early days of Elizabeth II's reign. The young queen takes her first steps as Queen, but finds herself torn between old tradition and those, like her husband, who want to modernise the monarchy

programme.title: The Windsors: Secrets of the Royal Tours
programme.desc: A look at official tours and state visits in the 1960s, exploring the changing role of the royal family's relationship to the Commonwealth and the political upheaval in the world as former colonies became independent, often with violent repercussions

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Police officer Hazel, 22, is rushed to St George's after crashing her motorbike while out on a ride with her father. Meanwhile, 86-year-old John is admitted to A&E with a suspected 'triple A' - a ruptured aortic aneurysm in his abdomen, a condition that can be fatal. The doctors must work quickly to diagnose the cause of the internal bleeding, but their investigations reveal further complications. John's children share the story of their father's life, and his surprising secret that sent shockwaves through the family

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, as Sean Lock and Claudia Winkleman take on Jon Richardson and Henning Wehn. Lexicographer Susie Dent is joined by Nick Helm to adjudicate in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: The Windsors: Inside the Royal Dynasty
programme.desc: This episode focuses on the early days of Elizabeth II's reign. The young queen takes her first steps as Queen, but finds herself torn between old tradition and those, like her husband, who want to modernise the monarchy

programme.title: The Windsors: Secrets of the Royal Tours
programme.desc: A look at official tours and state visits in the 1960s, exploring the changing role of the royal family's relationship to the Commonwealth and the political upheaval in the world as former colonies became independent, often with violent repercussions

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: In Scotland, Jimmy Doherty finds out how some whisky gets its smoky flavour. In Cyprus, Matt Tebbutt asks why halloumi cheese doesn't melt, and in France, Kate Quilton visits the world's biggest sweetcorn factory to find out how they get corn off the cob so neatly

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp enlists the help of paper-cutting expert Poppy Chancellor to make personalised wedding invitations for a couple about to tie the knot. Plus, the inventive presenter creates a craft collage to celebrate the arrival of a newborn baby

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate researchers struggle with an estate that involves a series of wills dating back to the 19th century, and when a vicar dies in Surrey, the team returns to Middlesbrough to delve into its steelmaking history. Plus, details of unclaimed estates for which heirs still need to be found

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo travel the length of Britain seeking items to fix up, flog on and sell for profit. Max upcycles a brass propeller into a stylish table lamp, while Henry goes all Victorian with an old pond yacht

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel Britain looking for items to fix up and flog on behalf of their owners. In this episode they're close to the Malvern Hills. Henry picks two saddles from horse owner Sabrina and converts them into bar stools, while Simon makes a handsome profit on a 1960s kitchen cupboard

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and mechanic Fuzz Townshend head to Croydon, south London, to rescue a star car that has been wallowing in its own garage for over 30 years. Its owner, Peter, is a serial petrolhead who has been fixing up motors for friends and family for most of his life, putting his own classic vehicle on the back burner. It's a 1964 Volvo P1800S, one of the most stylish cars to come off any production line, and the vehicle synonymous with cult 1960s TV series The Saint

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Cumbrian farmer Dennis has been pierced in the chest by a large metal spike. Motorcyclist Ian is in a critical condition after a crash in the Lake District. Plus, toddler Alistair is flown to a specialist burns unit after being badly scalded

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at the Old Ram Coaching Inn in Tivetshall St Mary, Norfolk, where Victoria MacDonald welcomes her guests to one of her three B&B sites

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Ye Old Crown in Edenbridge, Kent, where chef and owner Aaron Webster aims to deliver a three star service for a two star price

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at the Beach Lodge Hotel in Mablethorpe, Lincolnshire, as hosts Cheryl and Michele aim to offer their guests a perfect seaside break

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Alan and Alison play host to the others at the Farmer's Arms in Taunton, where they hope their palatial rooms will help them win. But a dusty radiator might be their undoing

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores, with Coaching Inn owner Victoria wanting to get to the bottom of Michele and Cheryl's comments about her toilet

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Most people would think twice about building on an extreme beachfront in Paekakariki, but it's a challenge top industrial designer Mark Pennington is ready to take on

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Micha and Crispin's beloved Bengal cat Loki has been hit by a car, while Faberge, an elderly Chinese crested dog with a fabulous wardrobe, is brought in by owners Julie and Suzanne after suffering seizures. Noel Fitzpatrick and the team also meet a deerhound called Willow, who has broken her neck, and with a high-risk operation to consider, her owners must decide whether to proceed

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel expand the project by setting up a luxury campsite on the chateau grounds, but they only have six weeks to complete it before the first guests arrive - and the weather is not on their side, with constant rain turning the planned site into a muddy field. They also have to contend with a visit from Dick's family at Easter

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Royal Marine captain Jon's life changed for ever when he stepped onto a land mine in Afghanistan and lost three limbs. After viewing many uninspiring specialist homes and considering awkward conversions, he and partner Becky realised their best option was to build a property themselves. The design is cutting-edge, with sleek glass walls, open-plan spaces and a dramatic wing-like roof, and the couple want there to be no noticeable adaptations. However, they soon use up their budget of £250,000 and encounter problems with their incredibly tight schedule. Kevin McCloud follows their progress

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Doctors investigate the underlying medical conditions that may have caused a motorist to crash into a lamppost, while the patient's son and his friend recount the influence she has had on both their lives. A man who fell during the night is treated for possible head trauma, and the father of a three-year-old patient reveals how his stepfather inspired him to be the best parent he can be

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, as team captain Sean Lock and Joe Wilkinson take on guest captain Richard Ayoade and Aisling Bea. Lexicographer Susie Dent is joined by Tom Allen to adjudicate in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A woman is airlifted to hospital after falling off her mobility scooter and getting knocked unconscious. Her daughter and husband discuss how she has led an active life despite being diagnosed with a crippling illness at a young age. A 78-year-old man is rushed into A&E after falling down the stairs, and doctors fear he may have a bleed on his brain

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics rush to save the life of a cyclist who has been crushed by a lorry in Leamington Spa, and in Coventry, a man is treated after falling from a barge

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, as team captain Sean Lock and Joe Wilkinson take on guest captain Richard Ayoade and Aisling Bea. Lexicographer Susie Dent is joined by Tom Allen to adjudicate in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Doctors investigate the underlying medical conditions that may have caused a motorist to crash into a lamppost, while the patient's son and his friend recount the influence she has had on both their lives. A man who fell during the night is treated for possible head trauma, and the father of a three-year-old patient reveals how his stepfather inspired him to be the best parent he can be

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty, Kate Quilton and Matt Tebbutt unveil more secrets about the world of mass-produced food. Jimmy heads to the US to find out how the big chunks in American ice-cream stay so crispy, Kate investigates a rise in olive oil prices and finds a disease wiping out olive trees in southern Italy, and Matt discovers why Bloody Marys are so popular on planes - and why airline food tastes very different at ground level

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Detroit Steel
programme.desc: Adam's friend turns up with a 1958 Mercury Turnpike Cruiser that is on its last leg, while Mobsteel step up their advertising when they strike a deal with the Detroit People Mover

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank organise a charity auction, and get a surprise when they visit a Kansas junkyard

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: US host Sean Kelly oversees UK bidders in Essex battling to win potential treasures found in storage containers, lock-ups and garages

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Sean Kelly and the bidders are in Alton, Hampshire, where Linda is concerned that the other bidders are taking advantage of her superior knowledge and her looks. Meanwhile, Boudicca and Heavy D form more than just a business partnership, and Barry impresses with his dainty footwork

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank explore a fellow picker's sheds and barns. The pair also examine the oddities collected by landowner John Atwater Bradley

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank find a huge sculpture of Elvis and trawl through a trailer full of rare art and military memorabilia

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Documentary with unrestricted access to America's Customs and Border Protection working on the bridges that separate Texas and Mexico, following the work of the many officers who police the bridges and keep America safe. In the first episode, a shipment of cardboard packaging hides a deadly secret, and heroin disguised as a laptop baffles the world's most advanced narcotic detection device

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May build their own stretch limousines out of other vehicles and use them to transport celebrities to the Brit Awards. Meanwhile, a Ford Mustang is put through its paces, and actress Billie Piper is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson attempts to build a car that is even smaller than the Peel P50 and then tests it out on the streets of London before pitching his concept in the Dragons' Den. James May puts the Bentley Continental GT Speed through its paces by taking it on a rally stage, while Richard Hammond drives the new Pagani Huayra hypercar before handing it over to the Stig. Actor Damian Lewis goes for a spin in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul
programme.desc: The chef continues his 900-mile road trip from Venice to Istanbul, sampling that array of gastronomic delights that lie on the route. In the penultimate episode Rick arrives in the Greek Peloponnese where he samples mouth-watering dishes such as rooster stew hilopites with mizithra cheese, and a home-made moussaka he claims is the best he have ever savoured. In his Symi island kitchen, Rick rolls up his sleeves to prepare galaktoboureko, a dessert of semolina custard in filo pastry, a favourite dish in Greece

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuiness, Chris Harris and Freddie Flintoff go on a road trip in a trio of affordable second-hand convertibles. On the test track, Chris puts the new Ariel Atom through its paces, while Freddie goes bungee jumping off a dam in an old Rover

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Comedian Romesh Ranganathan joins the team in the studio. Chris Harris races the £2.5million McLaren Speedtail against the RAF's latest fighter jet, and Paddy McGuinness tries out a cheap but luxurious off-roader

programme.desc: Lyndie Greenwood guests on the classic improv show, with Wayne Brady, Brad Sherwood, Colin Mochrie, Ryan Stiles, hosted by Aisha Tyler

programme.desc: Andi Oliver, Ivo Graham, Phill Jupitus and Kate Humble test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week, with a winner declared each Friday

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by actress and presenter Mel Giedroyc, heavyweight boxer David Haye, actor-musician Martin Kemp and comedian Romesh Ranganathan. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory and the dubious honour of being dubbed host Rob Brydon's liar of the week

programme.desc: Phill Jupitus, Cally Beaton and Rhod Gilbert join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig for an unconventional look at a potpourri of topics, while facing some impossible questions. programme.desc: The chef's gastronomic road trip reaches a conclusion as he arrives in Istanbul, just in time for the bluefish season, which allows him the chance to enjoy a fishing expedition on the Bosphorus, one of the world's most strategic waterways, which divides the city into two continents. Rick also cooks two much-loved local dishes - lavish kofte kebabs with pistachios, and kapuska, a hearty traditional Turkish cuisine stew from the Black Sea region, whose name is derived from the Russian word for cabbage

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness, Freddie Flintoff and Chris Harris take the latest sports cars from Ferrari, Aston Martin and Porsche on a Yorkshire road trip. Back on the track, Chris tests out Volkswagen's ID-R electric racer. YouTube star KSI is the studio guest

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness, Freddie Flintoff and Chris Harris put emergency vehicles to the test to see if the fire brigade, the police or the ambulance service has the fastest vehicles. Chris pays tribute to rally driver Colin McRae, Paddy puts the new BMW M8 through its paces, and comedian Tom Allen joins the team in the studio

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway? USA
programme.desc: Actress Lea Thompson guests on the classic improv show with Wayne Brady, Jonathan Mangum, Colin Mochrie, Ryan Stiles and hosted by Aisha Tyler

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway? USA
programme.desc: Aisha Tyler welcomes Katie Cassidy to the classic improv show, with regulars Wayne Brady, Brad Sherwood, Colin Mochrie and Ryan Stiles.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andi Oliver, Ivo Graham, Phill Jupitus and Kate Humble test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by radio presenter Sara Cox, comedian Jason Manford, journalist Nick Robinson and actor Harry Shearer, who aim to help the team captains deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry is joined by guests Sean Lock, David Mitchell, Danny Baker and regular panellist Alan Davies on the quiz with a difference. Correctness and even intelligence go out of the window as the host asks questions about hoaxes, and awards points for the answers he finds the most interesting

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sindhu Vee, Rhod Gilbert and Ed Byrne join regular panellist Alan Davies, as questionmaster Sandi Toksvig looks at pathology, the causal study of disease based on the analysis of bodily fluids. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window as the panellists attempt to answer the fiendishly difficult questions, with points invariably awarded for the answers the questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct

programme.title: Mel Giedroyc: Unforgivable
programme.desc: Comedy show in which the nation's favourite comics and celebrities compete to convince Mel that they are the most Unforgivable person in the room. In each episode three celebrities come clean and regale tales of their most despicable deeds and uncharitable behaviour as they are probed by host Mel Giedroyc and her assistant Lou Sanders for all the dastardly details. In this episode, Mel and Lou are joined by Graham Norton, Desiree Burch and Alex Brooker. Graham reveals a lie to his mother that went too far, and Alex talks about trying to catch a girl in a trap

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies sets challenges for Roisin Conaty, Tim Key, Romesh Ranganathan, Frank Skinner and Josh Widdicombe. They attempt to build a tower for an egg to see who can get it the highest and make a video 'blooper'

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Piece Hall in Halifax, the only surviving 18th-century cloth hall where textiles were once traded. Items featured include a sapphire that jewellery specialist John Benjamin thinks is the biggest he has ever seen - but which the owner thinks is fake. Other finds include a Chinese imperial robe, a mysterious set of 17th-century doors, and the oldest piece of glass to ever appear on the show

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: A look at the planet's most incredible metropolitan railways, and how they help people solve distinctly urban problems. Including a project to upgrade New York's transit system.

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: The undercover cameras reveal how animals often get up to mischief among themselves - and some are downright criminal. A colony of adelie penguins hunker down to avoid a storm, unaware one of them is a thief out to steal the stones that make up their nests, and Spy Tropicbird witnesses an aerial attack by aggressive frigate birds who attempt to steal his fish. A boisterous elephant has a temper tantrum in the mud near the waterhole, chimpanzees have extra-marital affairs and lemurs get high by sniffing toxic millipedes

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A formerly luxurious and strategic mountain-top villa on the island of Rhodes in the Mediterranean that's been ravaged by acts of revenge by locals since 1955

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo retraces railway journeys made by Queen Victoria in the south of England, where she would travel from Windsor Castle in Berkshire to her summer retreat on the Isle of Wight. The first leg of the trip takes him from Windsor's railway station to Didcot in Oxfordshire, stopping along the way to view a structure designed by Isambard Kingdom Brunel in Maidenhead. He also tries to collect the mail in the manner of Victorian postal workers on a steam-powered travelling post office

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's tour takes him to Alton, Hampshire, where he samples Victorian super food, explores Whitchurch Silk Mill, which has remained untouched for more than 150 years, and drives a steam train on the challenging Watercress Line

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey to Leeds by travelling through Buckinghamshire, meeting one of the Second World War's most secret agents at Bletchley. His last stop is Newport Pagnell, where he tackles the ancient craft of vellum-making

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The next part of Michael Portillo's journey from London to Leeds begins in Northampton, where Victorian methods have been used to make shoes for more than 130 years. programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

In the first episode, she reunited with former comedy partners Harry Enfield and Paul Whitehouse to discuss creating characters defined by wealth and status, and meets a wealthy businessman who grew up in poverty as a Romany gypsy,

programme.title: Big Dog Britain

Contributors include members of the Bones family and their Newfoundlands, the Mathers family and its wolfhounds, and the owners of Great Danes, English Mastiffs and St Bernards

programme.desc: Zoe Bailey hosts the first dinner party in Shropshire, and the Zumba instructor is hoping to entertain with a game of gargling while humming a tune. programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sheila Hancock and Gyles Brandreth steer a 72-foot vessel along the waterways of Staffordshire, journeying along the Caldon Canal and the Trent and Mersey Canal, to Stoke-on-Trent. They begin their journey at Cheddleton Flint Mill, built in 1253, hearing why flint was such an essential pottery ingredient. Gyles pays tribute to one of his great heroes, the Potteries-born writer Arnold Bennett, and Sheila visits the former home of one of her heroes, the ceramic artist Clarice Cliff

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: A steam engine enthusiast embarks on an ambitious coast-to-coast journey across Devon, travelling across the county's rolling hills in an 80-year-old steamroller with a top speed of eight miles per hour. Documentary exploring people's relationship with money, in which the actor, writer and director travels the country to meet people at both extremes of the wealth spectrum. In the first episode, she reunited with former comedy partners Harry Enfield and Paul Whitehouse to discuss creating characters defined by wealth and status, and meets a wealthy businessman who grew up in poverty as a Romany gypsy,

programme.desc: This edition focuses on Northamptonshire police's tackling of hate crime, following PCs Lauren El Sharkawi and Nina Crehan after they investigate a call from a family in Northampton who have been subjected to racist abuse from a neighbour. programme.desc: West Midlands paramedic Rob Armstrong and technician Mike Horton receive a call from a woman at a hotel who is about to give birth. In Birmingham, paramedic Rob Moore and technician Chloe Walker treat a two-year-old boy who is struggling to breathe, and in Coventry, paramedics Floss Corcoran and Orlaigh Barber help an elderly man who has suffered a blow to the head

They are supported by probation and police, doing the best they can, but large caseloads and a lack of resources leave officers overwhelmed - and when it's the job of probation to manage and assess the risk posed by offenders in the community, the consequences of mistakes can be disastrous

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

Can Jasmine Harman find a place that will be a sound investment and also give them and their family an idyllic holiday home for years to come, all for their £120,000 budget?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Childhood sweethearts Debbie and Tony Parkes have been married for 40 years, and are ready for a new chapter in their lives. With retirement around the corner, they've set their hearts on a holiday home in Albufeira on Portugal's Algarve with a budget of £80,000

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Salesman Richard Massey hosts the first party in Colchester, hoping his cheeky-chappy demeanour and signature dish Drunken Duck will be enough to see him walk away with the £1,000 prize at the end of the week-long competition

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: PR worker Darius G Laws hosts the second dinner party in Colchester, but knows he will have to work hard after being critical the previous evening. He creates a menu filled with local ingredients and home-grown produce, including his own blackberry wine, and hopes his Roman dress code will be enough to win him the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: American army wife Adrena Austin hosts the third dinner party in Colchester. She hopes her simple menu, which revisits her Armenian heritage, will be a welcome change for her guests, but while reactions to the main course are mixed, her entertainment proves a resounding success

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: James Coe throws the fourth party in Colchester, and his choice of menu proves popular from the starter onwards. However, Jodie Ezekiel finds his incessant discussion of money somewhat off-putting. Fortunately, the prospect of a dip in the host's hot tub improves moods, and gives Darius G Laws a chance to show off his pink pants

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Vegetarian Jodie Edzekiel hosts the final dinner party in Colchester, and tries to please her guests by serving them meat, provided she can bring herself to touch it. When Jodie's planned entertainment lets her down, she hopes a sing-a-long with her accompanying on the organ will save the day

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay is in New Jersey to help Rose Leone, who spent two years in a coma and came out of hospital to learn her son Michael had run her once-thriving Italian restaurant into the ground. With a menu of more than 126 dishes, the quality of the food is suffering, and even the chefs refuse to eat their own creations

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Ben and Billie continue to create their riverside glamping area by raising the roof of their dilapidated boathouse, while it's all hands on deck as Amy and Marc aim to complete their barn and cafe in time for hosting their first yoga class and celebratory meal

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Karl and Naomh, who first met at university, love the relaxed lifestyle of Menorca and want to be near the beautiful beaches of its south-east coast where they held their wedding. With Scarlette Douglas on hand and a top cash budget of £230,000, they are looking for a place with plenty of outside space for themselves and their two dogs

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi helps Teresa Lynch search for a property in the western Algarve, Portugal. With dramatically falling house prices, she hopes her budget of £120,000 will be enough to fulfil her lifelong dream of living on the country's coast

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Tom Allen and Liam Charles bring together the three best teams from both sets of heats, whose face challenge is to create 24 perfectly identical almond pithivier pies and individual savoury brioche, all in just three and a half hours. There, judges Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden ask them to produce a striking sugar showpiece, inspired by a futuristic jungle theme, along with two types of confectionery of their choice

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: A steam engine enthusiast embarks on an ambitious coast-to-coast journey across Devon, travelling across the county's rolling hills in an 80-year-old steamroller with a top speed of eight miles per hour. Farmers on Dartmoor rear a rare breed of sheep, while at Falmouth, a group of free divers explores a spectacular underwater cave

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: This edition focuses on Northamptonshire police's tackling of hate crime, following PCs Lauren El Sharkawi and Nina Crehan after they investigate a call from a family in Northampton who have been subjected to racist abuse from a neighbour. The police are also experiencing a surge in homophobic and transphobic abuse and are called to deal with one incident where the victim being targeted is just 13

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: West Midlands paramedic Rob Armstrong and technician Mike Horton receive a call from a woman at a hotel who is about to give birth. They rush through traffic as they try to get her to hospital before the baby is born in the back of the ambulance. In Birmingham, paramedic Rob Moore and technician Chloe Walker treat a two-year-old boy who is struggling to breathe, and in Coventry, paramedics Floss Corcoran and Orlaigh Barber help an elderly man who has suffered a blow to the head

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson welcomes two more singletons to choose a date from a selection of six naked people, who are revealed to them one body part at a time. Six-foot pansexual opera singer Clare is looking for a man as tall as she is, while Jessica, who has a disastrous dating history, hopes to bring out her naughty side

programme.title: Kathy Burke: Money Talks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary exploring people's relationship with money, in which the actor, writer and director travels the country to meet people at both extremes of the wealth spectrum. In the first episode, she reunited with former comedy partners Harry Enfield and Paul Whitehouse to discuss creating characters defined by wealth and status, and meets a wealthy businessman who grew up in poverty as a Romany gypsy,

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of Nathan and Amye as they build a giant cathedral-like property modelled on Dutch barn-style houses, but with a contemporary twist. It is a hugely ambitious plan, and with Nathan project managing and setting themselves just a year to finish the build, the pressure to pull it off is huge

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sheila Hancock and Gyles Brandreth steer a 72-foot vessel along the waterways of Staffordshire, journeying along the Caldon Canal and the Trent and Mersey Canal, to Stoke-on-Trent. They begin their journey at Cheddleton Flint Mill, built in 1253, hearing why flint was such an essential pottery ingredient. Gyles pays tribute to one of his great heroes, the Potteries-born writer Arnold Bennett, and Sheila visits the former home of one of her heroes, the ceramic artist Clarice Cliff

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart gets into trouble with the police for vandalising Flanders' Beatles memorabilia, so Marge sends him on an organised activity scheme to make sure he is supervised at all times. Unfortunately, Homer is appointed team leader, and it is not long before things start to fall apart

programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: Rich Kids Go Skint
programme.desc: A wealthy man with an expensive taste for designer clothes moves in with a Swansea single father on benefits, and has to get to grips with his dislike of animals

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: A crown green bowling team in Saydo is determined to end a losing streak, while Joe and Jan receive some devastating news concerning the future of the dog shelter. A Peterborough man who relocated to the Costa Del Sol launches his mobile cocktail bar, and a former racehorse that has been repeatedly rehomed and returned to the rescue centre may finally have found the right owner

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: Catching up with families featured on the programme in the past, providing an update and giving them a chance to share their hopes and ambitions for the future. Featuring Antony and James, who featured in the first episode of the series, along with Matt and Andy from season two. Plus, Waltham Forest's first-ever black bin lady Sharron and her son Marcus

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of medical emergency teams in the A&E department of Barnsley Hospital, beginning as severe weather conditions place it under even more stress than usual. Dr Julian tends to pensioner Brian who was brought in after breaking his ankle walking his dog in the snow, and the medic also sees former miner Andrew, who has a chronic lung condition. Paediatric consultant Dr Jane is called into action when four-year-old Teddy is rushed in after gashing his head in a sledging accident

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: The return after a break of the documentary following the work of officers from police forces across Yorkshire. Officers crack down on uninsured drivers whose recklessness raises everyone's premiums and kills more than 130 road users every year. In Halifax, a high-speed pursuit after one of these criminals ends in a devastating collision and foot race, while in Leeds a van courier is stopped, fails to deliver the correct paperwork to the cops, and then fails a drug test. Jamie Theakston presents

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: The police deal with a suicidal young man with mental health problems, an Eritrean woman dumped out of a lorry by people traffickers, and a local lass who has quarrelled with her two-timing boyfriend. Narrated by Jamie Theakston

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The Operational Support Unit has to evade a guard dog when carrying out a drugs raid on a farm, while a driver thinks he can pull a fast one by impersonating his brother. Barry finds himself dealing with an irate nightclubber, Craggy has an early morning encounter with a polecat and it is a case of deja vu for Dan as he deals with the same person twice in one shift

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Twenty officers descend on a hotel to arrest members of a criminal gang in their beds, Jon and Maya raid a cannabis farm, and Craggy and Phil catch a serial bacon snatcher. Three police cars and a helicopter take on a teenage van thief, Sgt Carlin stops a driver who has a gun hidden behind his seat, and Brian reduces a time-wasting motorist to a blubbering wreck

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Members of the operational support unit of Lincolnshire's Roads Policing Unit smash through a bedroom window in a drugs raid, a speeding driver is clocked at over 140mph, and friendships are pushed to the limit when amphetamines are found in a car. Plus, vehicle thieves try - and fail - to outpace the interceptors, and an officer gets help from a very unexpected source

programme.title: Dream Kitchens and Bathrooms with Mark Millar
programme.desc: Mark helps Aberdeenshire couple Emily and Chris, who want a traditional farmhouse style kitchen to be the heart of their cottage on their budget of £15,000. He also heads to Maidstone in Kent, where Camellia and Evan want a luxury five-star transformation of their bathroom for just £3,500

programme.title: RoboCop (2014)
programme.desc: A corporation that creates military robots seizes a chance to diversify into law enforcement when a Detroit cop is critically injured in a bombing. He is rebuilt as a crime-fighting cyborg, but struggles to regain his free will from his creators' control. Sci-fi thriller remake, starring Joel Kinnaman, Gary Oldman, Michael Keaton and Samuel L Jackson

programme.title: The Disappearance of Shannon Matthews
programme.desc: Concluding the documentary about the disappearance of nine-year-old Shannon Matthews in 2008, an investigation that gripped the nation. Her mother Karen claimed that her daughter had gone missing on the way home from school, prompting a nationwide search. But weeks later, it was revealed that Karen had known the truth all along - in a hoax kidnapping, Shannon was being kept hidden by a man known to her mother, who had plotted to claim the £50,000 reward money for her return

programme.title: Levi Bellfield: 5 Mistakes That Caught a Killer
programme.desc: An investigation into the case of doorman and car clamper Levi Bellfield, who committed three murders - including that of schoolgirl Milly Dowler in 2002. But would he have gone on to claim even more victims had he not made a string of mistakes that alerted police? Interviews with detectives, including the officer who interrogated Bellfield, as well as a former partner of the murderer, reveal the errors that led to his arrest

programme.title: Born to Kill?
programme.desc: Psychologists and criminology experts analyse the personality of American serial killer Dennis Rader, who was convicted in 2005 of murdering 10 people between 1974 and 1991. He became known as the 'BTK killer' after he offered it as a suggestion in a series of taunting letters to police and media - the letters standing for 'blind, torture, kill'. Through his own extraordinary testimony, the programme uncovers how this church deacon and scout leader led a double life as a sexual sadist and killer

programme.title: Aldi vs Lidl: Supermarket Wars
programme.desc: The success of the two German-owned supermarket chains, investigating their canny marketing ploys and aggressive sales techniques in their battle for domination. Insiders reveal the secrets behind both supermarkets' success and how they compete with their rivals, while the programme highlights the most tempting cut-price products that are the key to their success

programme.desc: New Saydo Park arrivals Carole and Phyllis already love life in Spain but they want to experience more of the community spirit, so they sign up for fellow resident Margaret's mystery tour. Where will it take them? Tree surgeon Rocky faces danger on a daily basis as he tends to the high branches of the Costa del Sol, while young magician Dean faces the first night of a residency at the Chamber of Secrets theatre

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: The Colemans from Salisbury swap homes, budgets and lives with the Morgan family from Hampshire, who are intrigued to know what life is like for the top 10-per-cent earners in the country. The Colemans have a large four-bedroom house, enjoy watersports and music festivals and have a weekly budget of about £1,800. The Morgans, meanwhile, who live in a second-floor flat, get by on £158 a week

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Staffing problems hit the department as several nurses call in sick, and young manager Tom has to use his charm to get the team to commit to working extra shifts. Paramedics are concerned about a family that were involved in a road traffic accident, and consultant Julian treats a critically ill patient with undiagnosed symptoms

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: This episode focuses on car thieves, with the hunt for a stolen Ford Fiesta in Leeds ending in a dramatic foot chase as officers pursue one of the mouthiest criminals they have ever encountered. On the other side of the city, two men steal two high-performance cars before leading the cops on one of the longest pursuits in the history of the force as they criss-cross the county for more than an hour, frequently at speeds in excess of 150mph

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents an edition featuring the dangerous, sometimes bizarre and always risky consequences of combining cars with night-time drinking. programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The team carries out a raid that turns up a stash of drugs, a weapon and a large pile of cash, while Jon Peacock is amazed to encounter a half-naked man covered in blood on the wintry streets of Lincoln, but the real surprise comes when the officer finds out what he has been up to. Suspicious driving leads Jim Woolerton to discover super-strength lager and drugs, and a speeding motorist comes out with a very middle-class excuse after being clocked travelling at 110mph

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Dan Brigginshaw tracks down a driver who has given him the slip on the side roads of Spalding, while Suzie Ranyard comes to the rescue of a motorist with a rather large passenger. programme.desc: Documentary examining the ongoing rivalry between the supermarkets. Marks & Spencer has always had a special place in the nation's heart, with almost 1,000 stores and a reputation for quality stretching back 135 years. But Waitrose has proved a fierce rival. However, both stores face tough challenges in the future, not least from online retailers. Which of the two is best equipped to weather the high-street storm?

programme.title: Mummy's Little Murderer
programme.desc: On May 7, 2011, 20-year-old Elliot Turner strangled his girlfriend Emily Longley and persuaded his doting parents to help him cover up the crime. This documentary examines events surrounding the murder, the investigation and the trial, and features interviews with family, friends, police and prosecutors, as well as covert recordings, surveillance footage and personal archive material

Ridley Scott's thriller sequel, starring Anthony Hopkins, Julianne Moore, Gary Oldman and Ray Liotta

programme.desc: Documentary in which journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. Nick also shows how detectives used laser-scanning technology to recreate a crime scene and close in on a gang that terrorised West Yorkshire pub owners in 2013

Taffy and Leanne have set up their own pool maintenance business - but it's their own pool that is causing them the biggest headache

programme.desc: The Hansen and Gittings families swap homes and budgets for the week - and although they both live in Bournemouth, their financial circumstances differ greatly. Yoko and Richard Hansen live in a five-bedroom house with their one child, take up to four overseas holidays a year and enjoy regular trips to see West End musicals. Their weekly budget is more than £1,300. Sarah and Greg Gittings, meanwhile, live in a two-bedroom flat, and get by on just £116 a week

programme.desc: The team are facing one of the busiest days in Barnsley Casualty's history, and the pressure mounts on staff members as patients await beds to become available, so self-named Emergency Clown Sister Jane helps to give the younger nurses a moral boost during this difficult period. programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Officers deal with a four-car collision outside a primary school. They make sure all those injured or hurt are accounted for, and also set about finding out who was to blame. He has left his passengers behind, but will they help the cops or hinder the course of the investigation? Jamie Theakston presents

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: North Yorkshire officers are called out to a massive crash near a theme park that has resulted in the death of at least one person. The need to understand what has happened and who - if anyone - is to blame tests the resources of all the traffic cops who attend and then investigate this fatal incident. Will their gut instincts that this is a drug-fuelled disaster prove correct?

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The dog unit embarks on a desperate manhunt amid fears that an armed and dangerous character is roaming the streets of Lincoln, while Craggy closes in on a suspected shoplifter with a bag full of surprises. programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.desc: Briscoe and Curtis re-examine a case closed since the 1960s after a vehicle containing the remains of a murdered man is dredged up from the Hudson River. Crime drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The chief suspect in the murder of several homeless men appears to be a schizophrenic street performer. Detectives Lupo and Bernard's problems compound when they find evidence suggesting a possible copycat is at work. Jeremy Sisto and Anthony Anderson star

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A respected financial analyst dies from an overdose of Viagra, a drug he was advised not to take. The subsequent investigation uncovers foul play involving his mistress (guest star Julia Roberts) and an unscrupulous tycoon, whose attorneys thwart McCoy and Carmichael's attempts to prosecute. Drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Sam Waterston

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A sound-system installer is murdered after supplying goods to a strip club covertly operated by the Mafia, and the finger of blame points to a small-time hoodlum whose crime boss father is serving life imprisonment. The case eventually comes to trial - but investigators are disturbed to hear an untrustworthy government attorney is handling the prosecution. Drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Steven Hill

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An army scientist goes missing, so Abby is asked to temporarily replace him on a top-secret research project - but Gibbs later learns her assignment is connected to a current murder investigation, and worries she may meet the same fate as her predecessor. Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the body of a marine is found strung up at a construction site, the agents travel to LA to work with another NCIS team on an investigation into an international arms deal that escalates into a high-priority case, and Tony begins to pry into Ziva's life. Guest starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J (NCIS: Los Angeles)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Agent Callen poses as an arms trade contact in a bid to catch killer Michael Aaron Rivkin. Meanwhile, Tony fears Ziva may not be loyal to the team, and a psychologist uncovers the truth about the relationship between Gibbs and Agent Macy. With Chris O'Donnell, Louise Lombard and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ex-FBI agent Tobias Fornell pleads with Gibbs to do whatever is necessary to take down drug dealers in the area after his daughter is hospitalised from an opiate overdose, leaving Gibbs feeling haunted by aspects of the case. Mark Harmon stars

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Stabler and Benson are assigned the case of a taxi driver found stabbed to death, and it soon emerges that the victim had a selection of false identities, leading the cops on a wild goose chase before they uncover any concrete leads. First episode of the spin-off drama, starring Mariska Hargitay, Christopher Meloni and Richard Belzer

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The officers are called in to investigate when a successful newspaper columnist falls six storeys to her death, dressed only in her underwear. It soon emerges her psychiatrist had visited the apartment that day for dinner, but he seems reluctant to tell the police what went on, making them believe he could be hiding vital clues. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: When the hostess of a rock 'n' roll chat show is found murdered in her home, a web of intrigue slowly reveals itself to Logan and Wheeler, linking the woman's death with an ageing musician who seems to be involved in a case of child abuse

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: A magician disappears during a live-burial trick and is later found dead miles away from the location of his performance. Goren and Eames' investigation takes them deep into the mysterious world of magic as they try to unravel the conjuror's final act to identify the murderer

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Nick and Monroe find a treasure in Germany that has been hidden for centuries, while back in Portland, Renard joins Hank and Wu to track down a deadly assassin

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: A local maskmaker targets Wesen to create Luchador masks that bestow great power to their wearers. As Nick, Hank, Monroe and Rosalee work to stop the killings, Renard considers an offer that will bring him closer to claiming his destiny. Meanwhile, Eve's further investigation of a recent incident raises new questions. Fantasy crime drama, starring David Giuntoli

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Curtis examine the murder of a popular Harlem community leader, while McCoy and Carmichael struggle to prevent a chaotic situation. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston, Jerry Orbach and Steven Hill

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Curtis hunt for the suspected killer of a parolee - but matters are complicated by the discovery that two bounty hunters are also pursuing their quarry. Acclaimed drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate the death of war veteran Greg Tanner, whose body was discovered in a Hudson University car park. Clues lead the duo to law professor Kevin Franklin, a former employee of the Department of Justice, and the picture gets clearer as Franklin's memos from the Bush Administration are leaked as the case goes to court. The first episode in the final season of the crime drama, starring Jeremy Sisto, Anthony Anderson and Sam Waterston

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A pregnant student is found murdered, and detectives Briscoe and Curtis discover she was strangled to death before being stabbed by the killer in an effort to make the attack seem like the random act of a psychopath. A mathematics professor soon comes under suspicion as eyewitnesses allege he was close to the crime scene on the night in question

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Part one of two. A shootout between a motorist and the police leads to the discovery of a body in a car boot - and puts the detectives on the trail of a money-laundering operation run by Russian gangsters. Drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is forced to work alongside the FBI to catch the killer of an Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent who was murdered during a poker game at the Secretary of the Navy's mansion. Elsewhere, Tony takes on Rivkin in a confrontation that ends in one of them being shot

programme.title: Entertainment News on 5
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team flies to Israel, but receives a hostile reception from Ziva's father, who is the head of intelligence agency Mossad. As events unfold, Gibbs learns that Ziva was romantically involved with Rivkin and finds himself forced to make a tough decision. Guest starring Michael Nouri, alongside Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony travels to North Africa on the trail of missing agent Ziva, who is thought to have been tracking down a terrorist leader called Saleem Ulman. Intent on exacting revenge for her apparent death during a flight to Somalia, he is taken hostage by militants and discovers she is still alive - only to be given a potentially catastrophic ultimatum. Starring Mark Harmon and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Goren and Eames uncover a series of frauds during an investigation into the deaths of several homeless people. Further inquiries lead them to a woman in the early stages of dementia and a man willing to commit murder to demonstrate his devotion to her. Starring Vincent D'Onofrio, Kathryn Erbe and Jamey Sheridan

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A teenage model is raped, assaulted with a hammer and left for dead outside a hospital, and is also found to have taken amphetamines. A photographer and his business partner are soon arrested for the crime - but finding a motive proves harder than anticipated. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Stabler and Benson investigate when the body of a murdered prostitute is found in Times Square, and are shocked at the insensitivity with which a cocky vice detective is handling the crime. Relieving him of duty, they soon link the woman's death to the murders of two other call girls, and start to believe a serial killer may be at work. Police drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: A boxer celebrating a victory is killed while trying to stop a bar brawl that has spilled out onto the street. programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: When the apparent suicide of a pupil at a prestigious private school turns out to be murder, Goren and Eames investigate the complex social hierarchy among the students and uncover an online community with secrets to hide. Crime drama, starring Vincent D'Onofrio and Kathryn Erbe

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Following a brutal attack in the woods, Nick and Hank stumble upon a terrifying Wesen disease that may be the inspiration for the modern-day werewolf myth. Meanwhile, Adalind is offered an uneasy alliance that may help her get back what she wants most and Eve's investigation uncovers a surprising revelation. Ann Cusack guest stars

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Nick and Hank suspect Wesen involvement after encountering a once-beautiful woman who seems to have aged 70 years overnight before dying. The investigation leads to a local doctor and Rosalee is enlisted to help find the truth. Meanwhile, Eve takes bold steps to learn more about Captain Renard's nefarious dealings. Fantasy crime drama, starring David Giuntoli, Russell Hornsby and Bree Turner

programme.title: Law & Order

Jerry Orbach and Sam Waterston star

programme.title: Law & Order

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Part two of two. As the case continues, Assistant DA Antonella Ricci and eyewitness Billy Woodson's mother are found dead at a supposedly safe location, killed by Colombian drug-dealers working alongside the Russian gangsters. Drama series, starring Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: When a man goes on a shooting spree in Central Park, Briscoe and new partner Green pull out all the stops to trace the weapon. Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay star

Acclaimed drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.desc: Family man Richard Cue, 57, has always felt grateful for the love and support he received from his adoptive parents, so he wants to find his birth mother to thank her for the sacrifice she made in giving him up in 1957. Having accessed his records, however, he has been searching for almost a decade with little success. Tania Bartlett's mother Julie fell pregnant to Iranian student Ahmed Kazem in the early 1960s, but the couple drifted apart when Julie felt unable to join Ahmed to start a new life in America. Despite intermittent contact with her father in the late 1970s and early 1980s, Tania has spent the past 30 years searching for him, not knowing if he is even alive. Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell bring these families back together

The wildlife presenter describes herself as 'a mongrel' - the result of her parents' lineages on the Caribbean islands of Trinidad and Martinique. Liz then heads to Martinique, where her paternal ancestors are said to have owned plantations - and slaves. However, what she uncovers is more complicated than she could have imagined

programme.title: The London Nobody Knows
programme.desc: Part one of two. Historian Dan Cruickshank retraces the steps taken by Geoffrey Fletcher in his 1960s guide to the city, The London Nobody Knows. He sets out to explore what remains of the capital 50 years on, visiting Hackney market with writer Iain Sinclair and Wilton's Music Hall

programme.title: Taste of Marley
programme.desc: Rohan Marley, son of legendary musician Bob Marley, reconnects with his roots as he goes on a journey to explore the food and music of his native Jamaica

programme.title: The Dales
programme.desc: Adrian Edmondson visits Kirkby Malham to meet members of the Wildman family, who have a rich farming heritage in the area. Father Chris is a fifth-generation butcher who plans to build his own charcuterie on the farm. The presenter also heads to Nidderdale, where sheep farmer Suzanne Benson reveals all about her new business of llama-trekking

programme.title: The Dales
programme.desc: Racehorse trainer Simon West begins to break in a new arrival from Ireland named Dolly. Plus, pregnant shepherdess Amanda Owen undertakes a 50-mile round trip to see the midwife and have her five-month scan, and Adrian Edmondson spends an evening with community choir the Buckden Singers

programme.title: One Good Turn (1954)
programme.desc: A boy living in an orphanage grows up to be the institution's lovable but clumsy caretaker. He must save the children's home from a wicked property developer who wants to build a factory on the site, and tricks him into taking the kids to the seaside so he can knock the building down. Comedy, starring Norman Wisdom and Joan Rice

programme.title: The October Man (1947)
programme.desc: A chemist with suicidal tendencies is falsely accused of murder - and his precarious mental state is further upset by taunts from the real killer. Psychological thriller, adapted from a novel by Eric Ambler, starring John Mills, Joan Greenwood and Joyce Carey

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tour of West Greenwich for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Danger Man
programme.desc: John Drake (Patrick McGoohan), a secret agent working for NATO, travels to different corners of the world in order to prevent crimes that threaten world peace

programme.title: The Dales
programme.desc: Farmer Alex Wilkes takes her small herd of dairy cows to compete at the Great Yorkshire Show in Harrogate, and Adrian Edmondson meets Tom Orde-Powlett, who is the heir to the historic Bolton Castle in Wensleydale. Plus, 16-year-old Phillip Mellin tries to shear sheep for the first time

programme.title: London Live News
programme.desc: News headlines, travel, weather and debate from across the capital

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Exploring the London's surprising and secret wildlife, including seals, parakeets, badgers, foxes and even pelicans who have made London their home. The programme also focuses on the admirers of these animals, from Billingsgate fish porters and Indian chefs to Crayfish Bob, who scours the canals for foreign species

programme.title: The Most Dangerous Ways to School
programme.desc: Children who take treacherous journeys to school, climbing up mountainous paths, swimming across rivers or fighting their way through icy wastelands

programme.title: Queensbury Boxing League
programme.desc: Boxers battle it out over three four and five two minute rounds against opponents - the most successful competitors will earn the right to challenge for the coveted Queensbury British title

programme.title: Bloodbath at the House of Death (1983)
programme.desc: Six scientists visit a country house that was once the site of a massacre to investigate psychic disturbances. They do not realise a short-tempered cult leader and his following of inept satanists have taken up residence, and intend to make sure no one leaves the estate alive. Horror spoof, starring Kenny Everett, Vincent Price and Pamela Stephenson

programme.title: Sophie's Choice (1982)
programme.desc: A Polish Holocaust survivor tries to start afresh in 1940s New York, but is tormented by ghosts from her nightmarish past, leaving her desperately seeking solace in a stormy relationship with an unpredictable chemist. But all is not as it seems, as a new neighbour seems set to discover. Drama, starring an Oscar-winning Meryl Streep alongside Kevin Kline and Peter MacNicol

programme.title: Relentless (2020)
programme.desc: An ex-convict must stay out of trouble for one more day to secure his freedom - but must choose between his principles and his future when he comes to the aid of a prostitute under threat from a gang. Action thriller, starring Steven Murphy and Tiffany-Ellen Robinson

programme.desc: A French woman living in London ends her relationship with her boyfriend when she decides to return to her own country. On the day she is due to leave, they meet again for one last goodbye. programme.desc: Ross Kemp examines the April 2015 heist in London's diamond district, when a group of career criminals broke into a safe deposit facility and stole approximately £14million in precious metals, cash and jewels, an event dubbed the biggest burglary of its type in British history. programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite teams made up of TV chefs, cooks and bakers to come up with the least likely answers to questions posed to the public. The contestants are Rustie Lee, Antony Worrall Thompson, Theo Randall, Rachel Allen, Jean-Christophe Novelli, Paul Rankin, Aaron Craze and John Whaite

programme.title: EFL Cup Highlights
programme.desc: Manchester United v Tottenham Hotspur. Action from the showpiece encounter at Wembley Stadium, where the first major trophy of the season was contested. Spurs were the holders, having defeated Chelsea 2-1 after extra time in last year's final, while the Red Devils had last lifted the cup in 2006 following a comfortable 4-0 win against Wigan Athletic

programme.title: SPFL Greatest Games
programme.desc: Motherwell v Hibernian from 2010

programme.title: EFL
programme.desc: Brentford v Yeovil Town. Action from the 2012/13 League One play-off final at Wembley Stadium, where the remaining place in the following season's Championship was decided. Brentford agonisingly missed out on automatic promotion on the final day of the regular campaign, but recovered to defeat Swindon Town on penalties in their semi-final, while Yeovil were 2-1 aggregate victors over Sheffield United

programme.title: EFL
programme.desc: Bradford City v Northampton Town. Action from the 2012/13 League Two play-off final at Wembley Stadium, where promotion to League One awaited the winners. The Bantams were making their second appearance of the season at Wembley having also reached the League Cup final following an outstanding giant-killing run, and they needed to come from behind to edge out Burton Albion to reach this stage. The Cobblers ended a run of recent poor form to win 1-0 in both legs of their semi-final against Cheltenham Town to move within one match of reaching the third tier of English football

programme.title: EFL
programme.desc: Crystal Palace v Watford. Action from the 2012/13 Championship play-off final at Wembley Stadium, as the teams contested a place in the top flight alongside the already promoted Cardiff City and Hull City. Palace reached this match when Wilfried Zaha scored a brace in a 2-0 away win against Brighton & Hove Albion after a goalless draw in the first leg. Meanwhile, Watford bounced back from narrowly missing out on automatic promotion by overcoming Leicester City in dramatic fashion, with Troy Deeney scoring an injury time-winner on the counter after Manuel Almunia had saved a penalty

programme.title: EFL Cup Highlights
programme.desc: Liverpool v Cardiff City. Action from the showpiece match at Wembley Stadium, where the winner of the first major trophy of the 2011/12 season was decided. The Reds had defeated the likes of Stoke City, Chelsea and Manchester City to reach the final and went into the match as clear favourites in their first visit to the national stadium since it was rebuilt. However, the Bluebirds, who defeated Crystal Palace on penalties in the semi-finals, had been in good form in the Championship and will have fancied their chances of producing an upset
